contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्रीललिता त्रिशतीस्तोत्र महामन्त्रस्य, भगवान् हयग्रीव ऋषिः, अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्रीललितामहात्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवता, ऐं बीजं, सौः शक्तिः, क्लीं कीलकं, मम चतुर्विधपुरुषार्थफलसिद्ध्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrīlalitā triśatīstōtra mahāmantrasya, bhagavān hayagrīva ṛṣiḥ, anuṣṭup Chandaḥ, śrīlalitāmahātripurasundarī dēvatā, aiṃ bījaṃ, sauḥ śaktiḥ, klīṃ kīlakaṃ, mama chaturvidhapuruṣārthaphalasiddhyarthē japē viniyōgaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Of this great mantra-stotra, the Śrī Lalitā Trishatī: the sage is Bhagavān Hayagrīva, the metre is Anuṣṭup, the presiding deity is Śrī Lalitā Mahātripurasundarī, the seed-syllable (bīja) is Aiṃ, the power (śakti) is Sauḥ, and the pin (kīlaka) is Klīṃ. It is recited for the attainment of the four-fold fruits of human life — dharma, artha, kāma, and mokṣa.
contentBlocks.meanings: अस्य (asya) – of this; श्रीललिता (śrīlalitā) – Śrī Lalitā; त्रिशतीस्तोत्र (triśatīstotra) – Trishati hymn (triśati:three hundred | stotra:hymn); महामन्त्रस्य (mahāmantrasya) – of the great mantra (mahā:great | mantrasya:of the mantra); भगवान् (bhagavān) – Lord; हयग्रीव (hayagrīva) – Hayagrīva; ऋषिः (ṛṣiḥ) – sage; अनुष्टुप् (anuṣṭup) – Anuṣṭup meter; छन्दः (chandaḥ) – meter; श्रीललितामहात्रिपुरसुन्दरी (śrīlalitāmahātripurasundarī) – Śrī Lalitā Mahātripurasundarī (śrī:auspicious | lalitā:Lalitā | mahā:great | tripura:three cities | sundarī:beautiful); देवता (devatā) – deity; ऐं (aiṃ) – Aiṃ (seed syllable); बीजं (bījaṃ) – seed syllable; सौः (sauḥ) – Sauḥ (power); शक्तिः (śaktiḥ) – power; क्लीं (klīṃ) – Klīṃ (pin); कीलकं (kīlakaṃ) – pin; मम (mama) – my; चतुर्विधपुरुषार्थफलसिद्ध्यर्थे (caturvidhapuruṣārthaphalasiddhyarthe) – for attaining four-fold fruits (catur:four | vidha:types | puruṣārtha:human goals | phala:fruits | siddhi:attainment | arthe:for the purpose of); जपे (jape) – in recitation; विनियोगः (viniyogaḥ) – application;

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यानम् ।
अतिमधुरचापहस्ता-
-मपरिमितामोदबाणसौभाग्याम् ।
अरुणामतिशयकरुणा-
-मभिनवकुलसुन्दरीं वन्दे ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyānam ।
atimadhurachāpahastā-
-maparimitāmōdabāṇasaubhāgyām ।
aruṇāmatiśayakaruṇā-
-mabhinavakulasundarīṃ vandē ।
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Her who holds a most sweet bow in Her hand, whose arrows carry limitless fragrance and beauty, who shines with a radiant rosy complexion, whose compassion is measureless, and who is the ever-fresh, ever-beautiful glory of the Kula tradition.
contentBlocks.meanings: ध्यानम् (dhyānam) – meditation; अतिमधुरचापहस्ता (atimadhuracāpahastā) – who holds a sweet bow (ati:very | madhura:sweet | cāpa:bow | hastā:hand); अपरिमितामोदबाणसौभाग्याम् (aparimitāmodabāṇasaubhāgyām) – whose arrows carry limitless fragrance (aparimita:limitless | āmoda:fragrance | bāṇa:arrows | saubhāgyām:beauty); अरुणामतिशयकरुणाम् (aruṇāmatiśayakaruṇām) – with radiant rosy compassion (aruṇa:rosy | atiśaya:excessive | karuṇām:compassion); अभिनवकुलसुन्दरीं (abhinavakulasundarīṃ) – ever-fresh Kula beauty (abhinava:ever-fresh | kula:Kula tradition | sundarīṃ:beauty); वन्दे (vande) – I bow;

contentBlocks.original:
अथ श्रीललितात्रिशती स्तोत्रम्श्री
श्री हयग्रीव उवाच ।
ककाररूपा कल्याणी कल्याणगुणशालिनी ।
कल्याणशैलनिलया कमनीया कलावती ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha śrīlalitātriśatī stotram
śrī hayagrīva uvācha ।
kakārarūpā kalyāṇī kalyāṇaguṇaśālinī ।
kalyāṇaśailanilayā kamanīyā kalāvatī ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kakārarūpā – She who embodies the form of the letter Ka, the first syllable of the Panchadashī
Kalyāṇī – The ever-auspicious one
Kalyāṇaguṇaśālinī – She who is adorned with all auspicious qualities
Kalyāṇaśailanilayā – She who dwells on the auspicious mountain
Kamanīyā – The supremely charming and desirable one
Kalāvatī – She who embodies all sixty-four arts
contentBlocks.meanings: श्री (śrī) – auspicious; हयग्रीव (hayagrīva) – Hayagriva; उवाच (uvāca) – said; ककाररूपा (kakārarūpā) – embodies form of 'Ka' (kakāra:letter 'Ka' | rūpā:form); कल्याणी (kalyāṇī) – ever-auspicious; कल्याणगुणशालिनी (kalyāṇaguṇaśālinī) – adorned with auspicious qualities (kalyāṇa:auspicious | guṇa:qualities | śālinī:adorned with); कल्याणशैलनिलया (kalyāṇaśailanilayā) – dwells on auspicious mountain (kalyāṇa:auspicious | śaila:mountain | nilayā:dwells); कमनीया (kamanīyā) – supremely charming; कलावती (kalāvatī) – embodies all arts;

contentBlocks.original:
कमलाक्षी कल्मषघ्नी करुणामृतसागरा ।
कदम्बकाननावासा कदम्बकुसुमप्रिया ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kamalākṣī kalmaṣaghnī karuṇāmṛtasāgarā ।
kadambakānanāvāsā kadambakusumapriyā ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kamalākṣī – She with beautiful lotus eyes
Kalmaṣaghnī – The destroyer of all impurities and sins
Karuṇāmṛtasāgarā – The ocean of compassion's nectar
Kadambakānanāvāsā – She who dwells in the kadamba forest
Kadambakusumapriyā – She who delights in kadamba blossoms
contentBlocks.meanings: कमलाक्षी (kamalākṣī) – She with beautiful lotus eyes (kamala:lotus | ākṣī:eyes); कल्मषघ्नी (kalmaṣaghnī) – The destroyer of impurities (kalmaṣa:impurities | ghnī:destroyer); करुणामृतसागरा (karuṇāmṛtasāgarā) – The ocean of compassion's nectar (karuṇa:compassion | amṛta:nectar | sāgarā:ocean); कदम्बकाननावासा (kadambakānanāvāsā) – She who dwells in kadamba forest (kadamba:kadamba | kānana:forest | vāsā:dwelling); कदम्बकुसुमप्रिया (kadambakusumapriyā) – She who delights in kadamba blossoms (kadamba:kadamba | kusuma:blossom | priyā:delighting in);

contentBlocks.original:
कन्दर्पविद्या कन्दर्पजनकापाङ्गवीक्षणा ।
कर्पूरवीटिसौरभ्यकल्लोलितककुप्तटा ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kandarpavidyā kandarpajanakāpāṅgavīkṣaṇā ।
karpūravīṭisaurabhyakallōlitakakuptaṭā ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kandarpavidyā – She who is the very science of love
Kandarpajanakāpāṅgavīkṣaṇā – She whose sidelong glance gives birth to Kāma, the god of love
Karpūravīṭisaurabhyakalloḷitakakuptaṭā – She whose camphor-scented betel fragrance wafts through all the directions
contentBlocks.meanings: कन्दर्पविद्या (kandarpavidyā) – science of love (kandarpa:love | vidyā:science); कन्दर्पजनकापाङ्गवीक्षणा (kandarpajanakāpāṅgavīkṣaṇā) – sidelong glance creates Kāma (kandarpa:Kāma, god of love | janaka:creates | apāṅga:sidelong glance | vīkṣaṇā:glance); कर्पूरवीटिसौरभ्यकल्लोलितककुप्तटा (karpūravīṭisaurabhyakallolitakakuptaṭā) – camphor-scented betel fragrance wafts (karpūra:camphor | vīṭi:betel | saurabhya:fragrance | kallolita:wafts | kakuptaṭā:through directions);

contentBlocks.original:
कलिदोषहरा कञ्जलोचना कम्रविग्रहा ।
कर्मादिसाक्षिणी कारयित्री कर्मफलप्रदा ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalidōṣaharā kañjalōchanā kamravigrahā ।
karmādisākṣiṇī kārayitrī karmaphalapradā ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kalidoṣaharā – She who removes the evils of the Kali age
Kañjalocanā – She with lotus eyes
Kamravigrahā – She with a supremely beautiful form
Karmādisākṣiṇī – The eternal witness of karma and all action
Kārayitrī – She who is the prime mover behind all activity
Karmaphalapradā – She who bestows the fruits of all actions
contentBlocks.meanings: कलिदोषहरा (kalidoṣaharā) – removes the evils of Kali (kali:Kali age | doṣa:evils | harā:remover); कञ्जलोचना (kañjalocanā) – she with lotus eyes (kañja:lotus | locanā:eyes); कम्रविग्रहा (kamravigrahā) – she with a beautiful form (kamra:beautiful | vigrahā:form); कर्मादिसाक्षिणी (karmādisākṣiṇī) – witness of karma and all actions (karma:karma | ādi:and others | sākṣiṇī:witness); कारयित्री (kārayitrī) – prime mover; कर्मफलप्रदा (karmaphalapradā) – bestows the fruits of all actions (karma:actions | phala:fruits | pradā:bestower);

contentBlocks.original:
एकाररूपा चैकाक्षर्येकानेकाक्षराकृतिः ।
एतत्तदित्यनिर्देश्या चैकानन्दचिदाकृतिः ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkārarūpā chaikākṣaryēkānēkākṣarākṛtiḥ ।
ētattadityanirdēśyā chaikānandachidākṛtiḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Ekārarūpā – She who embodies the form of the letter Ē, the second syllable of the Panchadashī
Ekākṣarī – She who is the single supreme syllable
Ekānekākṣarākṛtiḥ – She who manifests as both one syllable and many syllables
Etattadityanirdeśyā – She who is beyond all designation as 'this' or 'that'
Ekānandacidākṛtiḥ – She whose form is pure undivided bliss-consciousness
contentBlocks.meanings: एकाररूपा (ekārarūpā) – form of the letter Ē (eka:one | āra:letter Ē | rūpā:form); च (ca) – and; एकाक्षरी (ekākṣarī) – single supreme syllable (eka:one | akṣarī:syllable); एकानेकाक्षराकृतिः (ekānekākṣarākṛtiḥ) – manifests as one and many syllables (eka:one | aneka:many | akṣara:syllable | ākṛtiḥ:form); । (.) – None; एतत्तदित्यनिर्देश्या (etattadityanirdeśyā) – beyond designation as 'this' or 'that' (etat:this | tad:that | iti:thus | anirdeśyā:beyond designation); च (ca) – and; एकानन्दचिदाकृतिः (ekānandacidākṛtiḥ) – form of pure bliss-consciousness (eka:one | ānanda:bliss | cit:consciousness | ākṛtiḥ:form); ॥ (॥) – None; ५ (5) – 5;

contentBlocks.original:
एवमित्यागमाबोध्या चैकभक्तिमदर्चिता ।
एकाग्रचित्तनिर्ध्याता चैषणारहितादृता ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēvamityāgamābōdhyā chaikabhaktimadarchitā ।
ēkāgrachittanirdhyātā chaiṣaṇārahitādṛtā ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Evamityāgamābodhyā – She who is known through the Āgamas as 'thus' — the ineffable reality
Ekabhaktimadarchitā – She who is worshipped by those filled with single-pointed devotion
Ekāgrachittanirdhyātā – She who is meditated upon with a perfectly one-pointed mind
Eṣaṇārahitādṛtā – She who is cherished by those free from all selfish desires
contentBlocks.meanings: एवम् (evam) – thus; इति (iti) – as; आगम (āgama) – scriptures; अबोध्या (abodhyā) – known; च (ca) – and; एकभक्तिमत् (ekabhaktimat) – filled with single devotion (eka:single | bhaktimat:devotion-filled); अर्चिता (arcitā) – worshipped; एकाग्रचित्त (ekāgrachitta) – one-pointed mind (eka:one | agra:pointed | chitta:mind); निर्ध्याता (nirdhyātā) – meditated upon; च (ca) – and; एषणा (eṣaṇā) – desires; रहित (rahita) – free from; आदृता (ādṛtā) – cherished;

contentBlocks.original:
एलासुगन्धिचिकुरा चैनःकूटविनाशिनी ।
एकभोगा चैकरसा चैकैश्वर्यप्रदायिनी ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēlāsugandhichikurā chainaḥkūṭavināśinī ।
ēkabhōgā chaikarasā chaikaiśvaryapradāyinī ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Elāsugandhichikurā – She whose hair is fragrant with cardamom
Enaḥkūṭavināśinī – She who destroys accumulated heaps of sin
Ekabhogā – The supreme enjoyer, the sole experiencer of all
Ekarasā – She who is of one undivided essence
Ekaiśvaryapradāyinī – She who bestows singular supreme sovereignty
contentBlocks.meanings: एलासुगन्धिचिकुरा (elāsugandhicikurā) – She whose hair is fragrant with cardamom (elā:cardamom | sugandhi:fragrant | cikurā:hair); च (ca) – and; एनःकूटविनाशिनी (enaḥkūṭavināśinī) – She who destroys accumulated heaps of sin (enaḥ:sin | kūṭa:heap | vināśinī:destroyer); एकभोगा (ekabhogā) – The supreme enjoyer (eka:one | bhogā:enjoyer); च (ca) – and; एकरसा (ekarasā) – of one undivided essence (eka:one | rasā:essence); च (ca) – and; एकैश्वर्यप्रदायिनी (ekaiśvaryapradāyinī) – She who bestows singular supreme sovereignty (eka:one | aiśvarya:sovereignty | pradāyinī:bestower);

contentBlocks.original:
एकातपत्रसाम्राज्यप्रदा चैकान्तपूजिता ।
एधमानप्रभा चैजदनेकजगदीश्वरी ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkātapatrasāmrājyapradā chaikāntapūjitā ।
ēdhamānaprabhā chaijadanēkajagadīśvarī ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Ekātapatrasāmrājyapradā – She who bestows undivided sovereignty, as under a single royal parasol
Ekāntapūjitā – She who is worshipped with exclusive devotion
Edhamānaprabhā – She whose radiance ever grows and increases
Ejadanekajagadīśvarī – She who is sovereign of the countless worlds that tremble before Her
contentBlocks.meanings: एकातपत्रसाम्राज्यप्रदा (ekātapatrasāmrājyapradā) – bestows undivided sovereignty (eka:one | ātapatra:royal parasol | sāmrājya:sovereignty | pradā:bestows); च (ca) – and; एकान्तपूजिता (ekāntapūjitā) – worshipped with exclusive devotion (ekānta:exclusive | pūjitā:worshipped); एधमानप्रभा (edhamānaprabhā) – radiance ever grows (edhamāna:growing | prabhā:radiance); च (ca) – and; एजदनेकजगदीश्वरी (ejadanekajagadīśvarī) – sovereign of countless worlds (ejad:trembling | aneka:countless | jagat:worlds | īśvarī:sovereign);

contentBlocks.original:
एकवीरादिसंसेव्या चैकप्राभवशालिनी ।
ईकाररूपा चेशित्री चेप्सितार्थप्रदायिनी ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēkavīrādisaṃsēvyā chaikaprābhavaśālinī ।
īkārarūpā chēśitrī chēpsitārthapradāyinī ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Ekavīrādisaṃsevyā – She who is attended by Ekavīrā and other divine beings
Ekaprābhavaśālinī – She who alone possesses supreme glory and power
Īkārarūpā – She who embodies the form of the letter Ī, the third syllable of the Panchadashī
Īśitrī – The supreme controller and ruler of all
Īpsitārthadāyinī – She who grants all that is desired and sought
contentBlocks.meanings: एकवीरादिसंसेव्या (ekavīrādisaṃsevyā) – attended by Ekavīrā and others (eka:one | vīra:hero | ādi:and others | saṃsevyā:attended by); च (ca) – and; एकप्राभवशालिनी (ekaprābhavaśālinī) – possessing supreme glory (eka:one | prābhava:supreme glory | śālinī:possessing); ईकाररूपा (īkārarūpā) – form of the letter Ī (ī:letter Ī | kāra:form); च (ca) – and; ईशित्री (īśitrī) – supreme controller; च (ca) – and; इप्सितार्थप्रदायिनी (ipsitārthapradāyinī) – grants all desired (ipsita:desired | artha:object | pradāyinī:grants);

contentBlocks.original:
ईदृगित्यविनिर्देश्या चेश्वरत्वविधायिनी ।
ईशानादिब्रह्ममयी चेशित्वाद्यष्टसिद्धिदा ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īdṛgityavinirdēśyā chēśvaratvavidhāyinī ।
īśānādibrahmamayī chēśitvādyaṣṭasiddhidā ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Īdṛgityavinirdeśyā – She who is beyond all description and specification
Īśvaratvavidhāyinī – She who confers sovereignty and lordship upon Her devotees
Īśānādibrahmamayī – She who pervades all, from Īśāna down to Brahmā
Īśitvādyaṣṭasiddhidā – She who bestows the eight supernatural powers beginning with Īśitva
contentBlocks.meanings: ईदृगिति (īdṛgiti) – beyond description (īdṛk:such | iti:thus); अविनिर्देश्या (avinirdeśyā) – indescribable; च (ca) – and; ईश्वरत्वविधायिनी (īśvaratvavidhāyinī) – confers sovereignty (īśvaratva:sovereignty | vidhāyinī:conferring); ईशानादि (īśānādi) – from Īśāna to (īśāna:Īśāna | ādi:beginning with); ब्रह्ममयी (brahmamayī) – pervades all (brahma:Brahmā | mayī:consisting of); च (ca) – and; ईशित्व (īśitva) – Īśitva power; आद्य (ādya) – beginning with; अष्टसिद्धिदा (aṣṭasiddhidā) – bestows eight powers (aṣṭa:eight | siddhidā:bestowing powers);

contentBlocks.original:
ईक्षित्रीक्षणसृष्टाण्डकोटिरीश्वरवल्लभा ।
ईडिता चेश्वरार्धाङ्गशरीरेशाधिदेवता ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īkṣitrīkṣaṇasṛṣṭāṇḍakōṭirīśvaravallabhā ।
īḍitā chēśvarārdhāṅgaśarīrēśādhidēvatā ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Īkṣitrīkṣaṇasṛṣṭāṇḍakoṭiḥ – She who creates crores of universes by Her mere glance
Īśvaravallabhā – The beloved consort of Īśvara
Īḍitā – She who is praised and glorified by all
Īśvarārdhāṅgaśarīrā – She who forms the left half of Īśvara's body, the Ardhanārī form
Īśādhidēvatā – The presiding deity who governs even Śiva himself
contentBlocks.meanings: ईक्षित्रीक्षणसृष्टाण्डकोटिः (īkṣitrīkṣaṇasṛṣṭāṇḍakoṭiḥ) – creates crores of universes (īkṣitrī:by glance | īkṣaṇa:glance | sṛṣṭa:created | aṇḍa:universe | koṭiḥ:crores); ईश्वरवल्लभा (īśvaravallabhā) – beloved of Īśvara (īśvara:Īśvara | vallabhā:beloved); ईडिता (īḍitā) – praised and glorified; च (ca) – and; ईश्वरार्धाङ्गशरीरा (īśvarārdhāṅgaśarīrā) – half-body of Īśvara (īśvara:Īśvara | ardha:half | aṅga:body | śarīrā:body); ईशाधिदेवता (īśādhidēvatā) – presiding deity of Śiva (īśa:Śiva | adhi:presiding | devatā:deity);

contentBlocks.original:
ईश्वरप्रेरणकरी चेशताण्डवसाक्षिणी ।
ईश्वरोत्सङ्गनिलया चेतिबाधाविनाशिनी ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśvaraprēraṇakarī chēśatāṇḍavasākṣiṇī ।
īśvarōtsaṅganilayā chētibādhāvināśinī ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Īśvarapreraṇakarī – She who moves and motivates even Īśvara
Īśatāṇḍavasākṣiṇī – She who witnesses Śiva's cosmic dance
Īśvarotsaṅganilayā – She who resides in the lap of Īśvara
Ītibādhāvināśinī – She who destroys all afflictions and calamities
contentBlocks.meanings: ईश्वरप्रेरणकरी (īśvarapreraṇakarī) – She who motivates Īśvara (īśvara:Īśvara | preraṇa:motivation | karī:she who does); च (ca) – and; ईशताण्डवसाक्षिणी (īśatāṇḍavasākṣiṇī) – She who witnesses Śiva's dance (īśa:Śiva | tāṇḍava:cosmic dance | sākṣiṇī:she who witnesses); ईश्वरोत्सङ्गनिलया (īśvarotsaṅganilayā) – She who resides in Īśvara's lap (īśvara:Īśvara | utsaṅga:lap | nilayā:she who resides); च (ca) – and; इतिबाधाविनाशिनी (itibādhāvināśinī) – She who destroys afflictions (iti:thus | bādhā:afflictions | vināśinī:she who destroys);

contentBlocks.original:
ईहाविरहिता चेशशक्तिरीषत्स्मितानना ।
लकाररूपा ललिता लक्ष्मीवाणीनिषेविता ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īhāvirahitā chēśaśaktirīṣatsmitānanā ।
lakārarūpā lalitā lakṣmīvāṇīniṣēvitā ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Īhāvirahitā – She who is free from all desires and longing
Īśaśaktiḥ – She who is the very power and śakti of Śiva
Īṣatsmitānanā – She whose face wears a gentle and gracious smile
Lakārarūpā – She who embodies the form of the letter La, the fourth syllable of the Panchadashī
Lalitā – She who is graceful, playful, and eternally beautiful
Lakṣmīvāṇīniṣevitā – She who is attended and served by Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī
contentBlocks.meanings: ईहा (īhā) – desire; विरहिता (virahitā) – free from; च (ca) – and; ईशशक्ति (īśaśaktiḥ) – power of Śiva (īśa:Śiva | śaktiḥ:power); ईषत्स्मितानना (īṣatsmitānanā) – gentle smiling face (īṣat:gentle | smita:smiling | ānanā:face); लकाररूपा (lakārarūpā) – form of letter La (lakāra:letter La | rūpā:form); ललिता (lalitā) – graceful; लक्ष्मीवाणीनिषेविता (lakṣmīvāṇīniṣevitā) – served by Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī (lakṣmī:Lakṣmī | vāṇī:Sarasvatī | niṣevitā:served by);

contentBlocks.original:
लाकिनी ललनारूपा लसद्दाडिमपाटला ।
ललन्तिकालसत्फाला ललाटनयनार्चिता ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lākinī lalanārūpā lasaddāḍimapāṭalā ।
lalantikālasatphālā lalāṭanayanārchitā ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lākinī – She who is worshipped as Lākinī, presiding deity of the Maṇipūra chakra
Lalanārūpā – She who has the form of a graceful woman
Lasaddāḍimapāṭalā – She who is rosy-red like a shining pomegranate blossom
Lalantikālasatphālā – She whose forehead glitters with a jewelled pendant ornament
Lalāṭanayanārcitā – She who is worshipped by the eye on Śiva's forehead
contentBlocks.meanings: लाकिनी (lākinī) – She who is Lākinī; ललनारूपा (lalanārūpā) – form of a graceful woman (lalana:graceful woman | rūpā:form of); लसद्दाडिमपाटला (lasaddāḍimapāṭalā) – rosy-red like pomegranate blossom (lasat:shining | dāḍima:pomegranate | pāṭalā:rosy-red); ललन्तिकालसत्फाला (lalantikālasatphālā) – forehead glitters with ornament (lalantikā:forehead ornament | lasat:glitters | phālā:forehead); ललाटनयनार्चिता (lalāṭanayanārcitā) – worshipped by Śiva's forehead eye (lalāṭa:forehead | nayana:eye | arcitā:worshipped);

contentBlocks.original:
लक्षणोज्ज्वलदिव्याङ्गी लक्षकोट्यण्डनायिका ।
लक्ष्यार्था लक्षणागम्या लब्धकामा लतातनुः ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣaṇōjjvaladivyāṅgī lakṣakōṭyaṇḍanāyikā ।
lakṣyārthā lakṣaṇāgamyā labdhakāmā latātanuḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lakṣaṇojjvaladivyāṅgī – She whose divine limbs blaze with all auspicious marks
Lakṣakoṭyaṇḍanāyikā – The presiding goddess of lakhs of crores of universes
Lakṣyārthā – She who is the supreme goal, the ultimate meaning of all
Lakṣaṇāgamyā – She who is known through the indicators of the sacred scriptures
Labdhakāmā – She whose every desire is ever fulfilled
Latātanuḥ – She whose body is slender and graceful as a creeper
contentBlocks.meanings: लक्षणोज्ज्वलदिव्याङ्गी (lakṣaṇojjvaladivyāṅgī) – She whose divine limbs blaze (lakṣaṇa:marks | ujjvala:blazing | divya:divine | aṅgī:limbed); लक्षकोट्यण्डनायिका (lakṣakoṭyaṇḍanāyikā) – presiding goddess of universes (lakṣa:lakh | koṭi:crore | aṇḍa:universe | nāyikā:presiding goddess); लक्ष्यार्था (lakṣyārthā) – supreme goal (lakṣya:goal | artha:meaning); लक्षणागम्या (lakṣaṇāgamyā) – known through sacred indicators (lakṣaṇa:indicators | āgamya:known through); लब्धकामा (labdhakāmā) – whose desires are fulfilled (labdha:obtained | kāmā:desires); लतातनुः (latātanuḥ) – slender as a creeper (latā:creeper | tanuḥ:body);

contentBlocks.original:
ललामराजदलिका लम्बिमुक्तालताञ्चिता ।
लम्बोदरप्रसूर्लभ्या लज्जाढ्या लयवर्जिता ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lalāmarājadalikā lambimuktālatāñchitā ।
lambōdaraprasūrlabhyā lajjāḍhyā layavarjitā ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lalāmarājadalikā – She whose forehead is adorned with a beautiful lustrous mark
Lambimuktālatāñcitā – She who is adorned with hanging pearl necklaces
Lambodara-prasūḥ – The divine mother of Lambodara, the round-bellied Gaṇeśa
Labhyā – She who is attainable by sincere and devoted seekers
Lajjāḍhyā – She who is endowed with divine grace and modesty
Layavarjitā – She who is beyond dissolution, the eternal and indestructible one
contentBlocks.meanings: ललामराजदलिका (lalāmarājadalikā) – forehead adorned with mark (lalāma:forehead mark | rāja:king, beautiful | dalikā:adorned with); लम्बिमुक्तालताञ्चिता (lambimuktālatāñcitā) – adorned with pearl necklaces (lambi:hanging | muktā:pearls | latā:necklace | añcitā:adorned with); लम्बोदरप्रसूः (lambodara-prasūḥ) – mother of Lambodara (lambodara:Lambodara, Gaṇeśa | prasūḥ:mother); लभ्या (labhyā) – attainable; लज्जाढ्या (lajjāḍhyā) – endowed with modesty (lajjā:modesty | āḍhyā:endowed with); लयवर्जिता (layavarjitā) – beyond dissolution (laya:dissolution | varjitā:beyond);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकाररूपा ह्रींकारनिलया ह्रींपदप्रिया ।
ह्रींकारबीजा ह्रींकारमन्त्रा ह्रींकारलक्षणा ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārarūpā hrīṃkāranilayā hrīṃpadapriyā ।
hrīṃkārabījā hrīṃkāramantrā hrīṃkāralakṣaṇā ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārarūpā – She whose very form is the sacred bīja mantra Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāranilayā – She who dwells within the Hrīṃ mantra
Hrīṃpadapriyā – She who delights in the syllable Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārabījā – She for whom Hrīṃ is the seed syllable
Hrīṃkāramantrā – She who is the mantra Hrīṃ itself
Hrīṃkāralakṣaṇā – She who is known and characterized by Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकाररूपा (hrīṃkārarūpā) – She whose form is Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | rūpā:form); ह्रींकारनिलया (hrīṃkāranilayā) – She who dwells in Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | nilayā:dwelling); ह्रींपदप्रिया (hrīṃpadapriyā) – She who delights in Hrīṃ (hrīṃpada:syllable Hrīṃ | priyā:delights); ह्रींकारबीजा (hrīṃkārabījā) – Hrīṃ is the seed syllable (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | bījā:seed syllable); ह्रींकारमन्त्रा (hrīṃkāramantrā) – She who is the mantra Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | mantrā:mantra); ह्रींकारलक्षणा (hrīṃkāralakṣaṇā) – She characterized by Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | lakṣaṇā:characterized);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारजपसुप्रीता ह्रींमती ह्रींविभूषणा ।
ह्रींशीला ह्रींपदाराध्या ह्रींगर्भा ह्रींपदाभिधा ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārajapasuprītā hrīṃmatī hrīṃvibhūṣaṇā ।
hrīṃśīlā hrīṃpadārādhyā hrīṃgarbhā hrīṃpadābhidhā ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārajapasuprītā – She who is pleased by the repetition of the Hrīṃ mantra
Hrīṃmatī – She who possesses and embodies Hrīṃ
Hrīṃvibhūṣaṇā – She who is adorned with Hrīṃ as Her ornament
Hrīṃśīlā – She whose very nature is Hrīṃ
Hrīṃpadārādhyā – She who is worshipped through the syllable Hrīṃ
Hrīṃgarbhā – She within whose womb the universe of Hrīṃ resides
Hrīṃpadābhidhā – She who is named and known by the syllable Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारजपसुप्रीता (hrīṃkārajapasuprītā) – pleased by Hrīṃ mantra repetition (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | japa:repetition | suprītā:pleased); ह्रींमती (hrīṃmatī) – possesses and embodies Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | matī:possessing); ह्रींविभूषणा (hrīṃvibhūṣaṇā) – adorned with Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | vibhūṣaṇā:adorned); ह्रींशीला (hrīṃśīlā) – nature is Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | śīlā:nature); ह्रींपदाराध्या (hrīṃpadārādhyā) – worshipped through Hrīṃ syllable (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | pada:syllable | ārādhyā:worshipped); ह्रींगर्भा (hrīṃgarbhā) – womb contains universe of Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | garbhā:womb); ह्रींपदाभिधा (hrīṃpadābhidhā) – named by Hrīṃ syllable (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | pada:syllable | abhidhā:named);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारवाच्या ह्रींकारपूज्या ह्रींकारपीठिका ।
ह्रींकारवेद्या ह्रींकारचिन्त्या ह्रीं ह्रींशरीरिणी ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkāravāchyā hrīṃkārapūjyā hrīṃkārapīṭhikā ।
hrīṃkāravēdyā hrīṃkārachintyā hrīṃ hrīṃśarīriṇī ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkāravācyā – She who is expressed and denoted by Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārapūjyā – She who is worshipped through Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārapīṭhikā – She who is the sacred seat and pedestal of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāravedyā – She who is known and realised through Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārachintyā – She who is contemplated and meditated upon as Hrīṃ
Hrīṃ – She who IS the mantra Hrīṃ, the Māyā bīja itself
Hrīṃśarīriṇī – She whose body is constituted by the mantra Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारवाच्या (hrīṃkāravācyā) – expressed by Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | vācyā:expressed by); ह्रींकारपूज्या (hrīṃkārapūjyā) – worshipped through Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | pūjyā:worshipped through); ह्रींकारपीठिका (hrīṃkārapīṭhikā) – seat of Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | pīṭhikā:seat); ह्रींकारवेद्या (hrīṃkāravedyā) – known through Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | vedyā:known through); ह्रींकारचिन्त्या (hrīṃkāracintyā) – contemplated as Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | cintyā:contemplated as); ह्रीं (hrīṃ) – Hrīṃ; ह्रींशरीरिणी (hrīṃśarīriṇī) – body constituted by Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | śarīriṇī:body constituted by);

contentBlocks.original:
हकाररूपा हलधृक्पूजिता हरिणेक्षणा ।
हरप्रिया हराराध्या हरिब्रह्मेन्द्रवन्दिता ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hakārarūpā haladhṛkpūjitā hariṇēkṣaṇā ।
harapriyā harārādhyā haribrahmēndravanditā ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hakārarūpā – She whose form is the letter Ha, first syllable of the second kūṭa of the Panchadashī
Haladhṛkpūjitā – She who is worshipped by Balarāma, the wielder of the plough
Hariṇekṣaṇā – She with graceful, doe-like eyes
Harapriyā – She who is the beloved of Hara, Śiva
Harārādhyā – She who is worshipped by Hara himself
Haribrahmendravanditā – She who is bowed to by Viṣṇu, Brahmā, and Indra
contentBlocks.meanings: हकाररूपा (hakārarūpā) – She whose form is Ha (hakāra:letter Ha | rūpā:form); हलधृक्पूजिता (haladhṛkpūjitā) – worshipped by Balarāma (haladhṛk:Balarāma | pūjitā:worshipped); हरिणेक्षणा (hariṇekṣaṇā) – with doe-like eyes (hariṇa:doe | īkṣaṇā:eyes); हरप्रिया (harapriyā) – beloved of Hara (hara:Hara | priyā:beloved); हराराध्या (harārādhyā) – worshipped by Hara (hara:Hara | ārādhyā:worshipped); हरिब्रह्मेन्द्रवन्दिता (haribrahmendravanditā) – bowed to by Viṣṇu, Brahmā, Indra (hari:Viṣṇu | brahma:Brahmā | indra:Indra | vanditā:bowed to);

contentBlocks.original:
हयारूढासेविताङ्घ्रिर्हयमेधसमर्चिता ।
हर्यक्षवाहना हंसवाहना हतदानवा ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hayārūḍhāsēvitāṅghrirhayamēdhasamarchitā ।
haryakṣavāhanā haṃsavāhanā hatadānavā ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hayārūḍhāsevitāṅghriḥ – She whose feet are served by Hayārūḍhā, the horse-mounted goddess of Her retinue
Hayamedhasamarcitā – She who is worshipped in the Aśvamedha, the great horse sacrifice
Haryakṣavāhanā – She who rides upon the lion
Haṃsavāhanā – She who has the swan as Her vehicle
Hatadānavā – She who has vanquished and slain the demons
contentBlocks.meanings: हयारूढासेविताङ्घ्रिः (hayārūḍhāsevitāṅghriḥ) – served by Hayārūḍhā (haya:horse | ārūḍhā:mounted | sevitā:served | aṅghriḥ:feet); हयमेधसमर्चिता (hayamedhasamarcitā) – worshipped in Aśvamedha (haya:horse | medha:sacrifice | sam:together | arcitā:worshipped); हर्यक्षवाहना (haryakṣavāhanā) – rides upon the lion (hari:lion | yakṣa:spirit | vāhanā:vehicle); हंसवाहना (haṃsavāhanā) – swan as Her vehicle (haṃsa:swan | vāhanā:vehicle); हतदानवा (hatadānavā) – slain the demons (hata:slain | dānava:demons);

contentBlocks.original:
हत्यादिपापशमनी हरिदश्वादिसेविता ।
हस्तिकुम्भोत्तुङ्गकुचा हस्तिकृत्तिप्रियाङ्गना ॥ २२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hatyādipāpaśamanī haridaśvādisēvitā ।
hastikumbhōttuṅgakuchā hastikṛttipriyāṅganā ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hatyādipāpaśamanī – She who destroys even the greatest of sins, beginning with murder
Haridaśvādisevitā – She who is attended by Indra and the hosts of gods
Hastikumbhottuṅgakucā – She whose breasts are high and rounded like the frontal lobes of an elephant
Hastikṛttipriyāṅganā – She who is the cherished beloved of Śiva, who wears elephant hide
contentBlocks.meanings: हत्यादिपापशमनी (hatyādipāpaśamanī) – destroys sins like murder (hatyādi:beginning with murder | pāpa:sins | śamanī:destroyer); हरिदश्वादिसेविता (haridaśvādisēvitā) – attended by gods like Indra (haridaśva:Indra | ādi:and others | sēvitā:attended by); हस्तिकुम्भोत्तुङ्गकुचा (hastikumbhottuṅgakucā) – breasts high like elephant's (hasti:elephant | kumbha:frontal lobe | uttuṅga:high | kucā:breasts); हस्तिकृत्तिप्रियाङ्गना (hastikṛttipriyāṅganā) – beloved of Śiva with elephant hide (hasti:elephant | kṛtti:hide | priya:beloved | aṅganā:woman);

contentBlocks.original:
हरिद्राकुङ्कुमादिग्धा हर्यश्वाद्यमरार्चिता ।
हरिकेशसखी हादिविद्या हालामदालसा ॥ २३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
haridrākuṅkumādigdhā haryaśvādyamarārchitā ।
harikēśasakhī hādividyā hālāmadālasā ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Haridrākuṅkumādigdhā – She who is anointed with turmeric and vermilion
Haryaśvādyamarārcitā – She who is worshipped by Indra and all the immortals
Harikeśasakhī – She who is the dear companion of Harikeśa, Śiva
Hādividyā – She who is the Hādī Vidyā, the form of the Panchadashī beginning with Ha
Hālāmadālasā – She who is languid with the intoxication of divine bliss
contentBlocks.meanings: हरिद्राकुङ्कुमादिग्धा (haridrākuṅkumādigdhā) – anointed with turmeric and vermilion (haridrā:turmeric | kuṅkuma:vermilion | ādigdha:anointed with); हर्यश्वाद्यमरार्चिता (haryaśvādyamarārcitā) – worshipped by Indra and immortals (haryaśva:Indra | ādi:and others | amara:immortals | arcitā:worshipped by); हरिकेशसखी (harikeśasakhī) – dear companion of Harikeśa (harikeśa:Harikeśa, Śiva | sakhī:companion); हादिविद्या (hādividyā) – Hādī Vidyā (hādi:beginning with Ha | vidyā:knowledge); हालामदालसा (hālāmadālasā) – languid with divine bliss (hālā:divine bliss | mada:intoxication | ālasā:languid);

contentBlocks.original:
सकाररूपा सर्वज्ञा सर्वेशी सर्वमङ्गला ।
सर्वकर्त्री सर्वभर्त्री सर्वहन्त्री सनातना ॥ २४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakārarūpā sarvajñā sarvēśī sarvamaṅgalā ।
sarvakartrī sarvabhartrī sarvahantrī sanātanā ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sakārarūpā – She whose form is the letter Sa, opening the Sa group in the Panchadashī
Sarvajñā – The omniscient one who knows all
Sarveśī – The sovereign ruler of all
Sarvamaṅgalā – She who is auspiciousness itself, the welfare of all
Sarvakartrī – The creator of all
Sarvabhartrī – The sustainer and preserver of all
Sarvahantrī – The destroyer and dissolver of all
Sanātanā – The eternal one, without beginning or end
contentBlocks.meanings: सकाररूपा (sakārarūpā) – She whose form is Sa (sa:letter Sa | kāra:form | rūpā:having the form); सर्वज्ञा (sarvajñā) – omniscient (sarva:all | jñā:knowing); सर्वेशी (sarveśī) – sovereign ruler of all (sarva:all | īśī:ruler); सर्वमङ्गला (sarvamaṅgalā) – auspiciousness itself (sarva:all | maṅgalā:auspiciousness); सर्वकर्त्री (sarvakartrī) – creator of all (sarva:all | kartrī:creator); सर्वभर्त्री (sarvabhartrī) – sustainer of all (sarva:all | bhartrī:sustainer); सर्वहन्त्री (sarvahantrī) – destroyer of all (sarva:all | hantrī:destroyer); सनातना (sanātanā) – eternal;

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वानवद्या सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी सर्वसाक्षिणी ।
सर्वात्मिका सर्वसौख्यदात्री सर्वविमोहिनी ॥ २५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvānavadyā sarvāṅgasundarī sarvasākṣiṇī ।
sarvātmikā sarvasaukhyadātrī sarvavimōhinī ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sarvānavadyā – She who is perfectly without fault or blemish
Sarvāṅgasundarī – She who is beautiful in every limb
Sarvasākṣiṇī – The witness of all, ever-present in all
Sarvātmikā – She whose self is all — the universal Self
Sarvasaukhyadātrī – She who bestows happiness and wellbeing upon all
Sarvavimohini – She who enchants and captivates all creation
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वानवद्या (sarvānavadyā) – perfectly without fault (sarva:all | anavadya:without fault); सर्वाङ्गसुन्दरी (sarvāṅgasundarī) – beautiful in every limb (sarva:all | aṅga:limb | sundarī:beautiful); सर्वसाक्षिणी (sarvasākṣiṇī) – witness of all (sarva:all | sākṣiṇī:witness); सर्वात्मिका (sarvātmikā) – universal Self (sarva:all | ātmikā:self); सर्वसौख्यदात्री (sarvasaukhyadātrī) – bestows happiness upon all (sarva:all | saukhyadātrī:bestows happiness); सर्वविमोहिनी (sarvavimohinī) – enchants all creation (sarva:all | vimohinī:enchanting);

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वाधारा सर्वगता सर्वावगुणवर्जिता ।
सर्वारुणा सर्वमाता सर्वभूषणभूषिता ॥ २६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvādhārā sarvagatā sarvāvaguṇavarjitā ।
sarvāruṇā sarvamātā sarvabhūṣaṇabhūṣitā ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sarvādhārā – She who is the support and foundation of all existence
Sarvagatā – She who pervades all, present everywhere
Sarvāvaguṇavarjitā – She who is utterly free from all vices and shortcomings
Sarvāruṇā – She who is entirely of a rosy-red golden hue
Sarvamātā – The mother of all beings
Sarvabhūṣaṇabhūṣitā – She who is adorned with all ornaments
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वाधारा (sarvādhārā) – support of all (sarva:all | ādhārā:support); सर्वगता (sarvagatā) – pervades all (sarva:all | gatā:pervades); सर्वावगुणवर्जिता (sarvāvaguṇavarjitā) – free from all vices (sarva:all | avaguṇa:vices | varjitā:free from); सर्वारुणा (sarvāruṇā) – entirely rosy-red (sarva:entirely | aruṇā:rosy-red); सर्वमाता (sarvamātā) – mother of all (sarva:all | mātā:mother); सर्वभूषणभूषिता (sarvabhūṣaṇabhūṣitā) – adorned with all ornaments (sarva:all | bhūṣaṇa:ornaments | bhūṣitā:adorned with);

contentBlocks.original:
ककारार्था कालहन्त्री कामेशी कामितार्थदा ।
कामसञ्जीवनी कल्या कठिनस्तनमण्डला ॥ २७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kakārārthā kālahantrī kāmēśī kāmitārthadā ।
kāmasañjīvanī kalyā kaṭhinastanamaṇḍalā ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kakārārthā – She who is the very meaning and essence of the letter Ka
Kālahantrī – She who conquers and destroys time and death itself
Kāmeśī – The sovereign of Kāma, beloved consort of Kāmeśvara
Kāmitārthadā – She who grants all desired objects to Her devotees
Kāmasañjīvanī – She who revives and enlivens Kāma, the god of love
Kalyā – She who is skillful, wholesome, and auspicious
Kaṭhinastanamaṇḍalā – She whose bosom is firm and beautifully rounded
contentBlocks.meanings: ककारार्था (kakārārthā) – essence of the letter Ka (ka:letter Ka | ārthā:essence); कालहन्त्री (kālahantrī) – conqueror of time (kāla:time | hantrī:conqueror); कामेशी (kāmeśī) – sovereign of Kāma (kāma:Kāma | īśī:sovereign); कामितार्थदा (kāmitārthadā) – grants desired objects (kāmita:desired | artha:objects | dā:grants); कामसञ्जीवनी (kāmasañjīvanī) – revives Kāma (kāma:Kāma | sañjīvanī:revives); कल्या (kalyā) – auspicious; कठिनस्तनमण्डला (kaṭhinastanamaṇḍalā) – firm and rounded bosom (kaṭhina:firm | stana:bosom | maṇḍalā:rounded);

contentBlocks.original:
करभोरूः कलानाथमुखी कचजिताम्बुदा ।
कटाक्षस्यन्दिकरुणा कपालिप्राणनायिका ॥ २८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karabhōrūḥ kalānāthamukhī kachajitāmbudā ।
kaṭākṣasyandikaruṇā kapāliprāṇanāyikā ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Karabhōrūḥ – She whose thighs are smooth and rounded like the trunk of an elephant
Kalānāthamukhī – She whose face is as beautiful as the full moon
Kacajitāmbudā – She whose tresses surpass the dark rain clouds in beauty
Kaṭākṣasyandikaruṇā – She from whose sidelong glances flows an endless stream of compassion
Kapāliprāṇanāyikā – The beloved life-breath of Kapālī, the skull-bearing Śiva
contentBlocks.meanings: करभोरूः (karabhōrūḥ) – smooth, rounded thighs (karabha:elephant's trunk | ūrūḥ:thighs); कलानाथमुखी (kalānāthamukhī) – moon-faced (kalānātha:moon | mukhī:faced); कचजिताम्बुदा (kachajitāmbudā) – tresses surpass clouds (kaca:tresses | jitā:surpass | ambudā:clouds); कटाक्षस्यन्दिकरुणा (kaṭākṣasyandikaruṇā) – glances flow compassion (kaṭākṣa:glances | syandi:flow | karuṇā:compassion); कपालिप्राणनायिका (kapāliprāṇanāyikā) – beloved of Kapālī (kapāli:Kapālī | prāṇa:life-breath | nāyikā:beloved);

contentBlocks.original:
कारुण्यविग्रहा कान्ता कान्तिधूतजपावलिः ।
कलालापा कम्बुकण्ठी करनिर्जितपल्लवा ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāruṇyavigrahā kāntā kāntidhūtajapāvaliḥ ।
kalālāpā kambukaṇṭhī karanirjitapallavā ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kāruṇyavigrahā – She who is the very embodiment of compassion
Kāntā – The supremely beautiful and beloved one
Kāntidhūtajapāvaliḥ – She whose radiance surpasses a string of red japā flowers
Kalālāpā – She whose speech is sweet and melodious as music
Kambukaṇṭhī – She whose neck is graceful and conch-shaped
Karanirjitapallavā – She whose hands put to shame the most tender new leaves
contentBlocks.meanings: कारुण्यविग्रहा (kāruṇyavigrahā) – embodiment of compassion (kāruṇya:compassion | vigrahā:embodiment); कान्ता (kāntā) – beautiful and beloved; कान्तिधूतजपावलिः (kāntidhūtajapāvaliḥ) – radiance surpasses red japā flowers (kānti:radiance | dhūta:surpasses | japā:red japā flowers | valiḥ:string); कलालापा (kalālāpā) – sweet and melodious speech (kala:sweet | ālāpā:melodious speech); कम्बुकण्ठी (kambukaṇṭhī) – graceful conch-shaped neck (kambu:conch | kaṇṭhī:neck); करनिर्जितपल्लवा (karanirjitapallavā) – hands surpass tender new leaves (kara:hands | nirjita:surpass | pallavā:tender new leaves);

contentBlocks.original:
कल्पवल्लीसमभुजा कस्तूरीतिलकाञ्चिता ।
हकारार्था हंसगतिर्हाटकाभरणोज्ज्वला ॥ ३० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalpavallīsamabhujā kastūrītilakāñchitā ।
hakārārthā haṃsagatirhāṭakābharaṇōjjvalā ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kalpavallīsamabhujā – She whose arms are as graceful as the wish-fulfilling vine
Kastūrītilakāñcitā – She whose forehead is adorned with a fragrant musk-paste mark
Hakārārthā – She who is the meaning and essence of the letter Ha
Haṃsagatiḥ – She whose gait is graceful and stately as a swan's
Hāṭakābharaṇojjvalā – She who shines brilliantly adorned with golden ornaments
contentBlocks.meanings: कल्पवल्लीसमभुजा (kalpavallīsamabhujā) – arms like wish-fulfilling vine (kalpa:wish-fulfilling | vallī:vine | sama:like | bhujā:arms); कस्तूरीतिलकाञ्चिता (kastūrītilakāñcitā) – forehead adorned with musk mark (kastūrī:musk | tilaka:mark | añcitā:adorned); हकारार्था (hakārārthā) – essence of letter Ha (hakāra:letter Ha | arthā:essence); हंसगतिः (haṃsagatiḥ) – gait like a swan (haṃsa:swan | gatiḥ:gait); हाटकाभरणोज्ज्वला (hāṭakābharaṇojjvalā) – shining with golden ornaments (hāṭaka:golden | ābharaṇa:ornaments | ujjvalā:shining);

contentBlocks.original:
हारहारिकुचाभोगा हाकिनी हल्यवर्जिता ।
हरित्पतिसमाराध्या हठात्कारहतासुरा ॥ ३१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hārahārikuchābhōgā hākinī halyavarjitā ।
haritpatisamārādhyā haṭhātkārahatāsurā ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hārahārikucābhogā – She whose bosom is beautified by lovely necklaces
Hākinī – She who is worshipped as Hākinī, presiding deity of the Ājñā chakra
Halyavarjitā – She who is free from all that is to be abandoned, the sinless and pure one
Haritpatisamārādhyā – She who is devoutly worshipped by Indra, lord of the gods
Haṭhātkārahatāsurā – She who has slain demons with swift and decisive force
contentBlocks.meanings: हारहारिकुचाभोगा (hārahārikucābhogā) – beautified by lovely necklaces (hāra:necklace | hāri:lovely | kucābhogā:bosom beautified); हाकिनी (hākinī) – worshipped as Hākinī; हल्यवर्जिता (halyavarjitā) – free from sin (halya:sin | varjitā:free from); हरित्पतिसमाराध्या (haritpatisamārādhyā) – worshipped by Indra (harit:Indra | pati:lord | samārādhyā:devoutly worshipped); हठात्कारहतासुरा (haṭhātkārahatāsurā) – slain demons decisively (haṭhāt:swiftly | kāra:decisive | hatāsurā:slain demons);

contentBlocks.original:
हर्षप्रदा हविर्भोक्त्री हार्दसन्तमसापहा ।
हल्लीसलास्यसन्तुष्टा हंसमन्त्रार्थरूपिणी ॥ ३२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
harṣapradā havirbhōktrī hārdasantamasāpahā ।
hallīsalāsyasantuṣṭā haṃsamantrārtharūpiṇī ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Harṣapradā – She who bestows joy and bliss
Havirbhoktrī – She who partakes of the oblations offered in sacrifice
Hārdasantamasāpahā – She who removes the darkness of ignorance from the heart
Hallīsalāsyasantuṣṭā – She who is delighted by the Hallīsa, the joyful group dance
Haṃsamantrārtharūpiṇī – She whose form is the meaning of the Haṃsa mantra, the breath of life
contentBlocks.meanings: हर्षप्रदा (harṣapradā) – She who bestows joy (harṣa:joy | pradā:bestows); हविर्भोक्त्री (havirbhoktrī) – She who partakes of oblations (havis:oblations | bhoktrī:partakes); हार्दसन्तमसापहा (hārdasantamasāpahā) – She who removes heart's darkness (hārda:heart's | santamasa:darkness | āpahā:removes); हल्लीसलास्यसन्तुष्टा (hallīsalāsyasantuṣṭā) – Delighted by joyful dance (hallīsa:joyful | lāsya:dance | santuṣṭā:delighted); हंसमन्त्रार्थरूपिणी (haṃsamantrārtharūpiṇī) – Form of Haṃsa mantra's meaning (haṃsa:Haṃsa | mantra:mantra | artha:meaning | rūpiṇī:form);

contentBlocks.original:
हानोपादाननिर्मुक्ता हर्षिणी हरिसोदरी ।
हाहाहूहूमुखस्तुत्या हानिवृद्धिविवर्जिता ॥ ३३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hānōpādānanirmuktā harṣiṇī harisōdarī ।
hāhāhūhūmukhastutyā hānivṛddhivivarjitā ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hānopādānanirmuktā – She who is free from both loss and gain, beyond all taking and giving up
Harṣiṇī – She who is ever joyful and blissful
Harisodarī – She who is the sister of Hari, Viṣṇu
Hāhāhūhūmukhastutyā – She who is praised by the celestial Gandharvas Hāhā and Hūhū
Hānivṛddhivivarjitā – She who is beyond both decrease and increase, the unchanging absolute
contentBlocks.meanings: हानोपादाननिर्मुक्ता (hānopādānanirmuktā) – free from loss and gain (hāna:loss | upādāna:gain | nirmuktā:free from); हर्षिणी (harṣiṇī) – ever joyful; हरिसोदरी (harisodarī) – sister of Hari (hari:Hari | sodarī:sister); हाहाहूहूमुखस्तुत्या (hāhāhūhūmukhastutyā) – praised by Hāhā and Hūhū (hāhā:Hāhā | hūhū:Hūhū | mukha:face | stutyā:praised by); हानिवृद्धिविवर्जिता (hānivṛddhivivarjitā) – beyond decrease and increase (hāni:decrease | vṛddhi:increase | vivarjitā:beyond);

contentBlocks.original:
हय्यङ्गवीनहृदया हरिगोपारुणांशुका ।
लकाराख्या लतापूज्या लयस्थित्युद्भवेश्वरी ॥ ३४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hayyaṅgavīnahṛdayā harigōpāruṇāṃśukā ।
lakārākhyā latāpūjyā layasthityudbhavēśvarī ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hayyaṅgavīnahṛdayā – She whose heart is as tender and soft as freshly churned butter
Harigopāruṇāṃśukā – She who wears garments as red as the scarlet harigopa insect
Lakārākhyā – She who bears the name of the letter La in the third kūṭa of the Panchadashī
Latāpūjyā – She who is worshipped with offerings of flowering creepers
Layasthityudbhaveśvarī – The sovereign goddess of dissolution, preservation, and creation
contentBlocks.meanings: हय्यङ्गवीनहृदया (hayyaṅgavīnahṛdayā) – tender-hearted like butter (hayyaṅga:freshly churned butter | vīna:null | hṛdayā:heart); हरिगोपारुणांशुका (harigopāruṇāṃśukā) – wearing red garments (hari:scarlet insect | gopa:null | aruṇa:red | aṃśukā:garment); लकाराख्या (lakārākhyā) – named as letter La (lakāra:letter La | ākhyā:named); लतापूज्या (latāpūjyā) – worshipped with creepers (latā:creeper | pūjyā:worshipped); लयस्थित्युद्भवेश्वरी (layasthityudbhaveśvarī) – goddess of dissolution, preservation, creation (laya:dissolution | sthiti:preservation | udbhava:creation | īśvarī:goddess);

contentBlocks.original:
लास्यदर्शनसन्तुष्टा लाभालाभविवर्जिता ।
लङ्घ्येतराज्ञा लावण्यशालिनी लघुसिद्धिदा ॥ ३५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lāsyadarśanasantuṣṭā lābhālābhavivarjitā ।
laṅghyētarājñā lāvaṇyaśālinī laghusiddhidā ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lāsyadarśanasantuṣṭā – She who is delighted by the sight of the graceful Lāsya dance
Lābhālābhavivarjitā – She who is beyond both gain and loss
Laṅghyetarājñā – She whose command can never be transgressed
Lāvaṇyaśālinī – She who is endowed with supreme grace and loveliness
Laghusiddhidā – She who bestows supernatural powers quickly and easily
contentBlocks.meanings: लास्यदर्शनसन्तुष्टा (lāsyadarśanasantuṣṭā) – delighted by Lāsya dance (lāsya:Lāsya dance | darśana:sight | santuṣṭā:delighted); लाभालाभविवर्जिता (lābhālābhavivarjitā) – beyond gain and loss (lābha:gain | alābha:loss | vivarjitā:beyond); लङ्घ्येतराज्ञा (laṅghyetarājñā) – command never transgressed (laṅghya:transgressed | itarā:never | ājñā:command); लावण्यशालिनी (lāvaṇyaśālinī) – endowed with grace (lāvaṇya:grace | śālinī:endowed with); लघुसिद्धिदा (laghusiddhidā) – bestows powers quickly (laghu:quickly | siddhi:powers | dā:bestows);

contentBlocks.original:
लाक्षारससवर्णाभा लक्ष्मणाग्रजपूजिता ।
लभ्येतरा लब्धभक्तिसुलभा लाङ्गलायुधा ॥ ३६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lākṣārasasavarṇābhā lakṣmaṇāgrajapūjitā ।
labhyētarā labdhabhaktisulabhā lāṅgalāyudhā ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lākṣārasasavarṇābhā – She who shines with the deep red colour of lac dye
Lakṣmaṇāgrajapūjitā – She who is worshipped by Rāma, the elder brother of Lakṣmaṇa
Labhyetarā – She who is not easily attained, the rare and precious goal
Labdhabhaktisulabhā – She who becomes easily accessible to those who have attained devotion
Lāṅgalāyudhā – She who wields the plough as one of Her weapons
contentBlocks.meanings: लाक्षारससवर्णाभा (lākṣārasasavarṇābhā) – she who shines with lac dye (lākṣā:lac dye | rasa:essence | savarṇa:same color | ābhā:shine); लक्ष्मणाग्रजपूजिता (lakṣmaṇāgrajapūjitā) – worshipped by Rāma (lakṣmaṇa:Lakṣmaṇa | agraja:elder brother | pūjitā:worshipped); लभ्येतरा (labhyētarā) – not easily attained (labhya:attainable | itarā:other); लब्धभक्तिसुलभा (labdhabhaktisulabhā) – easily accessible to devotees (labdha:attained | bhakti:devotion | sulabhā:easily accessible); लाङ्गलायुधा (lāṅgalāyudhā) – wields the plough as weapon (lāṅgala:plough | āyudhā:weapon);

contentBlocks.original:
लग्नचामरहस्तश्रीशारदापरिवीजिता ।
लज्जापदसमाराध्या लम्पटा लकुलेश्वरी ॥ ३७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lagnachāmarahastaśrīśāradāparivījitā ।
lajjāpadasamārādhyā lampaṭā lakulēśvarī ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lagnacāmarahastaśrīśāradāparivījitā – She who is fanned by Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī holding golden chowries
Lajjāpadasamārādhyā – She who is worshipped in the form of Lajjā, the goddess of divine modesty
Lampaṭā – She who is lavishly generous in showering grace upon Her devotees
Lakulīśvarī – She who is the sovereign goddess of the Lakulīśa tradition
contentBlocks.meanings: लग्न (lagna) – attached; चामर (cāmara) – chowrie; हस्त (hasta) – hand; श्री (śrī) – Lakṣmī; शारदा (śāradā) – Sarasvatī; परिवीजिता (parivījitā) – fanned; लज्जा (lajjā) – modesty; पद (pada) – form; समाराध्या (samārādhyā) – worshipped; लम्पटा (lampaṭā) – lavishly generous; लकुलेश्वरी (lakuleśvarī) – sovereign goddess of Lakulīśa (lakula:Lakulīśa | īśvarī:sovereign goddess);

contentBlocks.original:
लब्धमाना लब्धरसा लब्धसम्पत्समुन्नतिः ।
ह्रींकारिणी ह्रींकाराद्या ह्रींमध्या ह्रींशिखामणिः ॥ ३८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labdhamānā labdharasā labdhasampatsamunnatiḥ ।
hrīṃkāriṇī hrīṃkārādyā hrīṃmadhyā hrīṃśikhāmaṇiḥ ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Labdhamānā – She who has attained all honour and glory
Labdharasā – She who has attained the supreme rasa, the essence of bliss
Labdhasampatsamunnatiḥ – She who has attained the highest prosperity and excellence
Hrīṃkāriṇī – She who manifests as and through Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārādyā – She who is at the very beginning of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃmadhyā – She who abides in the middle of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃśikhāmaṇiḥ – She who is the crown jewel at the summit of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: लब्धमाना (labdhamānā) – attained all honour; लब्धरसा (labdharasā) – attained supreme essence; लब्धसम्पत्समुन्नतिः (labdhasampatsamunnatiḥ) – attained highest prosperity (labdha:attained | sampat:prosperity | samunnatiḥ:highest excellence); ह्रींकारिणी (hrīṃkāriṇī) – manifests as Hrīṃ; ह्रींकाराद्या (hrīṃkārādyā) – beginning of Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | ādyā:beginning); ह्रींमध्या (hrīṃmadhyā) – middle of Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | madhyā:middle); ह्रींशिखामणिः (hrīṃśikhāmaṇiḥ) – crown jewel of Hrīṃ (hrīṃ:Hrīṃ | śikhā:summit | maṇiḥ:jewel);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारकुण्डाग्निशिखा ह्रींकारशशिचन्द्रिका ।
ह्रींकारभास्कररुचिर्ह्रींकाराम्भोदचञ्चला ॥ ३९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārakuṇḍāgniśikhā hrīṃkāraśaśichandrikā ।
hrīṃkārabhāskararuchirhrīṃkārāmbhōdachañchalā ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārakuṇḍāgniśikhā – She who is the flame of the sacred fire in the altar of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāraśaśicandrikā – She who is the moonlight streaming from the moon of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārabhāskararuciḥ – She who is the brilliance and light of the sun of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārāmbhodacañcalā – She who is the lightning dancing in the cloud of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारकुण्डाग्निशिखा (hrīṃkārakuṇḍāgniśikhā) – flame of Hrīṃ's altar (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | kuṇḍa:altar | agni:fire | śikhā:flame); ह्रींकारशशिचन्द्रिका (hrīṃkāraśaśicandrikā) – moonlight of Hrīṃ's moon (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | śaśi:moon | candrikā:moonlight); ह्रींकारभास्कररुचिः (hrīṃkārabhāskararuciḥ) – brilliance of Hrīṃ's sun (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | bhāskara:sun | ruciḥ:brilliance); ह्रींकाराम्भोदचञ्चला (hrīṃkārāmbhodacañcalā) – lightning in Hrīṃ's cloud (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | ambhoda:cloud | cañcalā:lightning);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारकन्दाङ्कुरिका ह्रींकारैकपरायणा ।
ह्रींकारदीर्घिकाहंसी ह्रींकारोद्यानकेकिनी ॥ ४० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārakandāṅkurikā hrīṃkāraikaparāyaṇā ।
hrīṃkāradīrghikāhaṃsī hrīṃkārōdyānakēkinī ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārakandāṅkurikā – She who is the tender sprout growing from the bulb of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāraikaparāyaṇā – She who is wholly devoted to and absorbed in Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāradīrghikāhaṃsī – She who is the graceful swan gliding on the lake of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārodyanakēkinī – She who is the peacock dancing in the garden of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारकन्दाङ्कुरिका (hrīṃkārakandāṅkurikā) – tender sprout from Hrīṃ bulb (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | kanda:bulb | aṅkurikā:tender sprout); ह्रींकारैकपरायणा (hrīṃkāraikaparāyaṇā) – devoted to Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | eka:one | parāyaṇā:devoted to); ह्रींकारदीर्घिकाहंसी (hrīṃkāradīrghikāhaṃsī) – swan on Hrīṃ lake (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | dīrghikā:lake | haṃsī:swan); ह्रींकारोद्यानकेकिनी (hrīṃkārōdyānakēkinī) – peacock in Hrīṃ garden (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | udyāna:garden | kēkinī:peacock);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारारण्यहरिणी ह्रींकारावालवल्लरी ।
ह्रींकारपञ्जरशुकी ह्रींकाराङ्गणदीपिका ॥ ४१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārāraṇyahariṇī hrīṃkārāvālavallarī ।
hrīṃkārapañjaraśukī hrīṃkārāṅgaṇadīpikā ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārāraṇyahariṇī – She who is the deer roaming the forest of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārāvālavallarī – She who is the creeper growing in the garden bed of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārapañjaraśukī – She who is the parrot in the cage of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārāṅgaṇadīpikā – She who is the lamp illuminating the courtyard of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारारण्यहरिणी (hrīṃkārāraṇyahariṇī) – deer in Hrīṃ forest (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | araṇya:forest | hariṇī:deer); ह्रींकारावालवल्लरी (hrīṃkārāvālavallarī) – creeper in Hrīṃ garden (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | āvāla:garden bed | vallarī:creeper); ह्रींकारपञ्जरशुकी (hrīṃkārapañjaraśukī) – parrot in Hrīṃ cage (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | pañjara:cage | śukī:parrot); ह्रींकाराङ्गणदीपिका (hrīṃkārāṅgaṇadīpikā) – lamp in Hrīṃ courtyard (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | aṅgaṇa:courtyard | dīpikā:lamp);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारकन्दरासिंही ह्रींकाराम्भोजभृङ्गिका ।
ह्रींकारसुमनोमाध्वी ह्रींकारतरुमञ्जरी ॥ ४२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārakandarāsiṃhī hrīṃkārāmbhōjabhṛṅgikā ।
hrīṃkārasumanōmādhvī hrīṃkāratarumañjarī ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārakandarāsiṃhī – She who is the lioness dwelling in the cave of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārāmbhojabhṛṅgikā – She who is the bee hovering over the lotus of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārasumanomādhvī – She who is the sweet nectar of the flowers of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāratarumaṅjarī – She who is the blossoming cluster of flowers on the tree of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारकन्दरासिंही (hrīṃkārakandarāsiṃhī) – lioness in Hrīṃ cave (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | kandara:cave | asiṃhī:lioness); ह्रींकाराम्भोजभृङ्गिका (hrīṃkārāmbhojabhṛṅgikā) – bee over Hrīṃ lotus (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | ambhoja:lotus | bhṛṅgikā:bee); ह्रींकारसुमनोमाध्वी (hrīṃkārasumanomādhvī) – nectar of Hrīṃ flowers (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | sumanaḥ:flowers | mādhvī:nectar); ह्रींकारतरुमञ्जरी (hrīṃkāratarumañjarī) – blossom on Hrīṃ tree (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | taru:tree | mañjarī:blossom);

contentBlocks.original:
सकाराख्या समरसा सकलागमसंस्तुता ।
सर्ववेदान्ततात्पर्यभूमिः सदसदाश्रया ॥ ४३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakārākhyā samarasā sakalāgamasaṃstutā ।
sarvavēdāntatātparyabhūmiḥ sadasadāśrayā ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sakārākhyā – She who bears the name of the letter Sa in the third kūṭa of the Panchadashī
Samarasā – She who is of perfectly uniform, undivided essence — the state of supreme equipoise
Sakalāgamasaṃstutā – She who is praised and glorified by all the Āgamas
Sarvavēdāntatātparyabhūmiḥ – She who is the ultimate ground and purport of all Vedānta
Sadasadāśrayā – She who is the refuge and substratum of both the existent and the non-existent
contentBlocks.meanings: सकाराख्या (sakārākhyā) – named with letter Sa (sa:letter Sa | ākhyā:named); समरसा (samarasā) – of uniform essence; सकलागमसंस्तुता (sakalāgamasaṃstutā) – praised by all Āgamas (sakala:all | āgama:Āgamas | saṃstutā:praised); सर्ववेदान्ततात्पर्यभूमिः (sarvavēdāntatātparyabhūmiḥ) – ground of all Vedānta (sarva:all | vēdānta:Vedānta | tātparya:purport | bhūmiḥ:ground); सदसदाश्रया (sadasadāśrayā) – refuge of existent and non-existent (sat:existent | asat:non-existent | āśrayā:refuge);

contentBlocks.original:
सकला सच्चिदानन्दा साध्या सद्गतिदायिनी ।
सनकादिमुनिध्येया सदाशिवकुटुम्बिनी ॥ ४४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakalā sachchidānandā sādhyā sadgatidāyinī ।
sanakādimunidhyēyā sadāśivakuṭumbinī ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sakalā – She who is the whole, the undivided and complete reality
Saccidānandā – She who is Sat-Cit-Ānanda — pure Being, Consciousness, and Bliss
Sādhyā – She who is the supreme goal to be attained
Sadgatidāyinī – She who grants the highest liberation and the auspicious path
Sanakādimunidhyeyā – She who is meditated upon by Sanaka and the other great sages
Sadāśivakuṭumbinī – She who is the beloved consort of Sadāśiva, the eternal Śiva
contentBlocks.meanings: सकला (sakalā) – whole, complete; सच्चिदानन्दा (saccidānandā) – pure Being, Consciousness, Bliss (sat:being | cit:consciousness | ānanda:bliss); साध्या (sādhyā) – supreme goal; सद्गतिदायिनी (sadgatidāyinī) – grants highest liberation (sat:highest | gati:path, liberation | dāyinī:grants); सनकादिमुनिध्येया (sanakādimunidhyeyā) – meditated upon by sages (sanaka:Sanaka | ādi:and others | muni:sages | dhyeyā:meditated upon); सदाशिवकुटुम्बिनी (sadāśivakuṭumbinī) – consort of Sadāśiva (sadāśiva:Sadāśiva | kuṭumbinī:consort);

contentBlocks.original:
सकालाधिष्ठानरूपा सत्यरूपा समाकृतिः ।
सर्वप्रपञ्चनिर्मात्री समनाधिकवर्जिता ॥ ४५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sakālādhiṣṭhānarūpā satyarūpā samākṛtiḥ ।
sarvaprapañchanirmātrī samanādhikavarjitā ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sakāladhiṣṭhānarūpā – She who is the substratum and ground of all time and all things
Satyarūpā – She whose very form is Truth, the absolute reality
Samākṛtiḥ – She whose form is perfect in equanimity and balance
Sarvaprapañcanirmātrī – She who creates the entire phenomenal world
Samanādhikavarjitā – She who has no equal and no superior
contentBlocks.meanings: सकालाधिष्ठानरूपा (sakālādhiṣṭhānarūpā) – substratum of all time (sa:with | kāla:time | adhiṣṭhāna:substratum | rūpā:form); सत्यरूपा (satyarūpā) – form of truth (satya:truth | rūpā:form); समाकृतिः (samākṛtiḥ) – perfect form (sama:equanimity | ākṛtiḥ:form); सर्वप्रपञ्चनिर्मात्री (sarvaprapañcanirmātrī) – creator of all worlds (sarva:all | prapañca:worlds | nirmātrī:creator); समनाधिकवर्जिता (samanādhikavarjitā) – without equal or superior (sama:equal | anādhika:superior | varjitā:without);

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वोत्तुङ्गा सङ्गहीना सगुणा सकलेष्टदा ।
ककारिणी काव्यलोला कामेश्वरमनोहरा ॥ ४६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvōttuṅgā saṅgahīnā saguṇā sakalēṣṭadā ।
kakāriṇī kāvyalōlā kāmēśvaramanōharā ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Sarvottuṅgā – She who is the highest of all, the supreme summit of existence
Saṅgahīnā – She who is without attachment or association of any kind
Saguṇā – She who takes on attributes and qualities for the sake of devotees
Sakaleṣṭadā – She who grants all that is desired to all beings
Kakāriṇī – She who is again of the nature of Ka, the beginning of the final kūṭa
Kāvyalolā – She who delights in poetry and literary beauty
Kāmeśvaramanoharā – She who captivates and enchants the mind of Kāmeśvara
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वोत्तुङ्गा (sarvottuṅgā) – highest of all (sarva:all | uttuṅgā:highest); सङ्गहीना (saṅgahīnā) – without attachment (saṅga:attachment | hīnā:without); सगुणा (saguṇā) – with attributes (sa:with | guṇā:attributes); सकलेष्टदा (sakalēṣṭadā) – grants all desires (sakala:all | iṣṭa:desired | dā:grants); ककारिणी (kakāriṇī) – nature of Ka (ka:Ka | kāriṇī:nature of); काव्यलोला (kāvyalolā) – delights in poetry (kāvya:poetry | lolā:delights in); कामेश्वरमनोहरा (kāmeśvaramanoharā) – enchants Kāmeśvara's mind (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | manoharā:enchants the mind);

contentBlocks.original:
कामेश्वरप्राणनाडी कामेशोत्सङ्गवासिनी ।
कामेश्वरालिङ्गिताङ्गी कामेश्वरसुखप्रदा ॥ ४७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmēśvaraprāṇanāḍī kāmēśōtsaṅgavāsinī ।
kāmēśvarāliṅgitāṅgī kāmēśvarasukhapradā ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kāmeśvaraprāṇanāḍī – She who is the very life-nerve and vital force of Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśotsaṅgavāsinī – She who dwells in the lap of Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvarāliṅgitāṅgī – She whose body is tenderly embraced by Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvarasukhapradā – She who bestows supreme joy and bliss upon Kāmeśvara
contentBlocks.meanings: कामेश्वरप्राणनाडी (kāmeśvaraprāṇanāḍī) – life-nerve of Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | prāṇa:life | nāḍī:nerve); कामेशोत्सङ्गवासिनी (kāmeśotsaṅgavāsinī) – dwells in Kāmeśvara's lap (kāmeśa:Kāmeśvara | utsaṅga:lap | vāsinī:dwelling); कामेश्वरालिङ्गिताङ्गी (kāmeśvarāliṅgitāṅgī) – embraced by Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | āliṅgita:embraced | aṅgī:body); कामेश्वरसुखप्रदा (kāmeśvarasukhapradā) – bestows joy to Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | sukha:joy | pradā:bestows);

contentBlocks.original:
कामेश्वरप्रणयिनी कामेश्वरविलासिनी ।
कामेश्वरतपःसिद्धिः कामेश्वरमनःप्रिया ॥ ४८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmēśvarapraṇayinī kāmēśvaravilāsinī ।
kāmēśvaratapaḥsiddhiḥ kāmēśvaramanaḥpriyā ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kāmeśvaraprāṇayinī – She who is the deeply devoted and loving partner of Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvaravilāsinī – She who sports and plays joyfully with Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvaratapaḥsiddhiḥ – She who is the fruit and fulfilment of Kāmeśvara's austerities
Kāmeśvaramanaḥpriyā – She who is most dear to the heart of Kāmeśvara
contentBlocks.meanings: कामेश्वरप्रणयिनी (kāmeśvarapraṇayinī) – devoted partner of Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | praṇayinī:devoted partner); कामेश्वरविलासिनी (kāmeśvaravilāsinī) – joyful with Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | vilāsinī:joyful); कामेश्वरतपःसिद्धिः (kāmeśvaratapaḥsiddhiḥ) – fulfilment of Kāmeśvara's austerities (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | tapaḥ:austerities | siddhiḥ:fulfilment); कामेश्वरमनःप्रिया (kāmeśvaramanaḥpriyā) – dear to Kāmeśvara's heart (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | manaḥ:heart | priyā:dear);

contentBlocks.original:
कामेश्वरप्राणनाथा कामेश्वरविमोहिनी ।
कामेश्वरब्रह्मविद्या कामेश्वरगृहेश्वरी ॥ ४९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmēśvaraprāṇanāthā kāmēśvaravimōhinī ।
kāmēśvarabrahmavidyā kāmēśvaragṛhēśvarī ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kāmeśvaraprāṇanāthā – She who is the beloved mistress and life of Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvaravimohinī – She who enchants and captivates Kāmeśvara himself
Kāmeśvarabrahmavidyā – She who is the supreme knowledge of Brahman revealed by Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvaragṛheśvarī – She who is the sovereign mistress of Kāmeśvara's divine abode
contentBlocks.meanings: कामेश्वरप्राणनाथा (kāmeśvaraprāṇanāthā) – beloved mistress of Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | prāṇa:life | nāthā:mistress); कामेश्वरविमोहिनी (kāmeśvaravimohinī) – enchantress of Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | vimohinī:enchantress); कामेश्वरब्रह्मविद्या (kāmeśvarabrahmavidyā) – supreme knowledge of Brahman (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | brahma:Brahman | vidyā:knowledge); कामेश्वरगृहेश्वरी (kāmeśvaragṛheśvarī) – sovereign mistress of Kāmeśvara's abode (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | gṛha:abode | īśvarī:mistress);

contentBlocks.original:
कामेश्वराह्लादकरी कामेश्वरमहेश्वरी ।
कामेश्वरी कामकोटिनिलया काङ्क्षितार्थदा ॥ ५० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāmēśvarāhlādakarī kāmēśvaramahēśvarī ।
kāmēśvarī kāmakōṭinilayā kāṅkṣitārthadā ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Kāmeśvarāhlādakarī – She who fills Kāmeśvara with delight and joy
Kāmeśvaramahīśvarī – She who is the great sovereign over Kāmeśvara
Kāmeśvarī – She who is the ruler of desire, the goddess Kāmeśvarī
Kāmakoṭinilayā – She who dwells at Kāmakoṭi, the sacred seat at Kanchipuram
Kāṅkṣitārthadā – She who grants all that is yearned for and hoped for
contentBlocks.meanings: कामेश्वराह्लादकरी (kāmeśvarāhlādakarī) – She who fills Kāmeśvara with delight (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | āhlādakarī:delighting); कामेश्वरमहेश्वरी (kāmeśvaramahīśvarī) – She who is the great sovereign over Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvara:Kāmeśvara | mahīśvarī:great sovereign); कामेश्वरी (kāmeśvarī) – ruler of desire; कामकोटिनिलया (kāmakoṭinilayā) – She who dwells at Kāmakoṭi (kāmakoṭi:Kāmakoṭi | nilayā:dwelling); काङ्क्षितार्थदा (kāṅkṣitārthadā) – She who grants all that is yearned for (kāṅkṣita:yearned for | arthadā:grants);

contentBlocks.original:
लकारिणी लब्धरूपा लब्धधीर्लब्धवाञ्छिता ।
लब्धपापमनोदूरा लब्धाहङ्कारदुर्गमा ॥ ५१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakāriṇī labdharūpā labdhadhīrlabdhavāñChitā ।
labdhapāpamanōdūrā labdhāhaṅkāradurgamā ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Lakāriṇī – She who is of the nature of La in the final Sa-Ka-La-Hrīṃ kūṭa
Labdharūpā – She who has manifested a form, the self-revealed one
Labdhadhīḥ – She who has attained supreme wisdom and discernment
Labdhavāñcitā – She who has attained everything that is wished for
Labdhapāpamanodūrā – She who is far removed from all sinful thoughts
Labdhāhaṅkāradurgamā – She who is inaccessible to those bound by ego and pride
contentBlocks.meanings: लकारिणी (lakāriṇī) – nature of La; लब्धरूपा (labdharūpā) – manifested a form; लब्धधीः (labdhadhīḥ) – attained supreme wisdom; लब्धवाञ्छिता (labdhavāñchitā) – attained wished for; लब्धपापमनोदूरा (labdhapāpamanodūrā) – far from sinful thoughts (labdha:attained | pāpa:sinful | manaḥ:mind | dūrā:far); लब्धाहङ्कारदुर्गमा (labdhāhaṅkāradurgamā) – inaccessible to ego-bound (labdha:attained | ahaṅkāra:ego | durgamā:inaccessible);

contentBlocks.original:
लब्धशक्तिर्लब्धदेहा लब्धैश्वर्यसमुन्नतिः ।
लब्धवृद्धिर्लब्धलीला लब्धयौवनशालिनी ॥ ५२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labdhaśaktirlabdhadēhā labdhaiśvaryasamunnatiḥ ।
labdhavṛddhirlabdhalīlā labdhayauvanaśālinī ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Labdhaśaktiḥ – She who has attained infinite power
Labdhadehā – She who has taken on a body, the beautifully embodied one
Labdhaiśvaryasamunnatiḥ – She who has attained the summit of all sovereignty
Labdhavṛddhiḥ – She who has attained eternal growth and abundance
Labdhalīlā – She who has attained the fullness of Her divine play
Labdhayauvanaśālinī – She who is endowed with the splendour of eternal youth
contentBlocks.meanings: लब्धशक्तिः (labdhaśaktiḥ) – attained infinite power (labdha:attained | śaktiḥ:power); लब्धदेहा (labdhadehā) – taken on a body (labdha:attained | dehā:body); लब्धैश्वर्यसमुन्नतिः (labdhaiśvaryasamunnatiḥ) – attained summit of sovereignty (labdha:attained | aiśvarya:sovereignty | samunnatiḥ:summit); लब्धवृद्धिः (labdhavṛddhiḥ) – attained eternal growth (labdha:attained | vṛddhiḥ:growth); लब्धलीला (labdhalīlā) – attained divine play (labdha:attained | līlā:play); लब्धयौवनशालिनी (labdhayauvanaśālinī) – endowed with eternal youth (labdha:attained | yauvana:youth | śālinī:endowed with);

contentBlocks.original:
लब्धातिशयसर्वाङ्गसौन्दर्या लब्धविभ्रमा ।
लब्धरागा लब्धपतिर्लब्धनानागमस्थितिः ॥ ५३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labdhātiśayasarvāṅgasaundaryā labdhavibhramā ।
labdharāgā labdhapatirlabdhanānāgamasthitiḥ ॥ 53 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Labdhātiśayasarvāṅgasundaryā – She who has attained surpassing beauty in every single limb
Labdhavibhramā – She who has attained captivating grace and irresistible charm
Labdharāgā – She who has attained the deep red hue of divine love and passion
Labdhapatiḥ – She who has attained Kāmeśvara as Her eternal Lord
Labdhanānāgamasthitiḥ – She who has attained the supreme state described in all the Āgamas
contentBlocks.meanings: लब्धातिशयसर्वाङ्गसौन्दर्या (labdhātiśayasarvāṅgasaundaryā) – She who has attained surpassing beauty (labdha:attained | atiśaya:surpassing | sarva:all | aṅga:limbs | saundaryā:beauty); लब्धविभ्रमा (labdhavibhramā) – She who has attained captivating grace (labdha:attained | vibhramā:captivating grace); लब्धरागा (labdharāgā) – She who has attained divine love (labdha:attained | rāgā:divine love); लब्धपतिः (labdhapatiḥ) – She who has attained Kāmeśvara (labdha:attained | patiḥ:Lord); लब्धनानागमस्थितिः (labdhanānāgamasthitiḥ) – She who has attained supreme state (labdha:attained | nānā:various | āgama:scriptures | sthitiḥ:state);

contentBlocks.original:
लब्धभोगा लब्धसुखा लब्धहर्षाभिपूरिता ।
ह्रींकारमूर्तिर्ह्रींकारसौधशृङ्गकपोतिका ॥ ५४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labdhabhōgā labdhasukhā labdhaharṣābhipūritā ।
hrīṃkāramūrtirhrīṃkārasaudhaśṛṅgakapōtikā ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Labdhabhogā – She who has attained the fullness of all experience and enjoyment
Labdhasukhā – She who has attained supreme happiness and contentment
Labdhaharṣābhipūritā – She who is filled to the brim with attained joy
Hrīṃkāramūrtiḥ – She whose entire form is constituted by Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārasaudhaśṛṅgakapotikā – She who is the dove perched on the pinnacle of the palace of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: लब्धभोगा (labdhabhogā) – attained fullness of experience (labdha:attained | bhogā:fullness of experience); लब्धसुखा (labdhasukhā) – attained supreme happiness (labdha:attained | sukhā:supreme happiness); लब्धहर्षाभिपूरिता (labdhaharṣābhipūritā) – filled with attained joy (labdha:attained | harṣa:joy | abhipūritā:filled to the brim); ह्रींकारमूर्तिः (hrīṃkāramūrtiḥ) – form constituted by Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | mūrtiḥ:form); ह्रींकारसौधशृङ्गकपोतिका (hrīṃkārasaudhaśṛṅgakapotikā) – dove on Hrīṃ palace pinnacle (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | saudha:palace | śṛṅga:pinnacle | kapotikā:dove);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारदुग्धाब्धिसुधा ह्रींकारकमलेन्दिरा ।
ह्रींकारमणिदीपार्चिर्ह्रींकारतरुशारिका ॥ ५५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkāradugdhābdhisudhā hrīṃkārakamalēndirā ।
hrīṃkāramaṇidīpārchirhrīṃkārataruśārikā ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkāradugdhābdhisudhā – She who is the nectar rising from the milk-ocean of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārakamalendrā – She who is Lakṣmī seated in the lotus of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāramaṇidīpārciḥ – She who is the flame of the gem-studded lamp of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārataruśārikā – She who is the myna bird singing in the tree of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारदुग्धाब्धिसुधा (hrīṃkāradugdhābdhisudhā) – nectar from Hrīṃ's milk-ocean (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | dugdha:milk | abdhi:ocean | sudhā:nectar); ह्रींकारकमलेन्दिरा (hrīṃkārakamalēndirā) – Lakṣmī in Hrīṃ's lotus (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | kamala:lotus | indirā:Lakṣmī); ह्रींकारमणिदीपार्चिः (hrīṃkāramaṇidīpārciḥ) – flame of Hrīṃ's gem-lamp (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | maṇi:gem | dīpa:lamp | arciḥ:flame); ह्रींकारतरुशारिका (hrīṃkārataruśārikā) – myna in Hrīṃ's tree (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | taru:tree | śārikā:myna);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारपेटकमणिर्ह्रींकारादर्शबिम्बिता ।
ह्रींकारकोशासिलता ह्रींकारास्थाननर्तकी ॥ ५६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārapēṭakamaṇirhrīṃkārādarśabimbitā ।
hrīṃkārakōśāsilatā hrīṃkārāsthānanartakī ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārapetakamaṇiḥ – She who is the priceless jewel in the casket of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārādarśabimbitā – She who is beautifully reflected in the mirror of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārakoṣāsilatā – She who is the sword-creeper springing from the sheath of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārāsthānanartakī – She who dances in the royal assembly hall of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारपेटकमणिः (hrīṃkārapēṭakamaṇiḥ) – priceless jewel in Hrīṃ casket (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | pēṭaka:casket | maṇiḥ:jewel); ह्रींकारादर्शबिम्बिता (hrīṃkārādarśabimbitā) – reflected in Hrīṃ mirror (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | ādarśa:mirror | bimbitā:reflected); ह्रींकारकोशासिलता (hrīṃkārakōśāsilatā) – sword-creeper from Hrīṃ sheath (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | kōśa:sheath | āsilatā:sword-creeper); ह्रींकारास्थाननर्तकी (hrīṃkārāsthānanartakī) – dances in Hrīṃ assembly hall (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ | sthāna:assembly hall | nartakī:dancer);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारशुक्तिकामुक्तामणिर्ह्रींकारबोधिता ।
ह्रींकारमयसौवर्णस्तम्भविद्रुमपुत्रिका ॥ ५७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkāraśuktikāmuktāmaṇirhrīṃkārabōdhitā ।
hrīṃkāramayasauvarṇastambhavidrumaputrikā ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkāraśuktikāmuktāmaṇiḥ – She who is the precious pearl within the oyster shell of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārabodhitā – She who is awakened and illumined by Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāramayasauvarṇastambhavidrumaputrikā – She who is the coral doll upon the golden pillar made of the Hrīṃ mantra
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकार (hrīṃkāra) – Hrīṃ mantra; शुक्तिका (śuktikā) – oyster shell; मुक्ता (muktā) – pearl; मणिः (maṇiḥ) – gem; ह्रींकारबोधिता (hrīṃkārabodhitā) – awakened by Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | bodhitā:awakened); ह्रींकारमय (hrīṃkāramaya) – made of Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ mantra | maya:made of); सौवर्ण (sauvarṇa) – golden; स्तम्भ (stambha) – pillar; विद्रुम (vidruma) – coral; पुत्रिका (putrikā) – doll;

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारवेदोपनिषद् ह्रींकाराध्वरदक्षिणा ।
ह्रींकारनन्दनारामनवकल्पकवल्लरी ॥ ५८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkāravēdōpaniṣad hrīṃkārādhvaradakṣiṇā ।
hrīṃkāranandanārāmanavakalpakavallarī ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkāravedopaniṣat – She who is the Upaniṣad and the very essence of the Vedas within Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārādhvaradakṣiṇā – She who is the sacred gift offered in the great sacrifice of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāranandanārāmanavakalpakavallarī – She who is the ever-fresh wish-fulfilling creeper in the pleasure garden of Hrīṃ
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारवेदोपनिषद् (hrīṃkāravedopaniṣad) – essence of Vedas within Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | veda:Vedas | upaniṣad:essence); ह्रींकाराध्वरदक्षिणा (hrīṃkārādhvaradakṣiṇā) – sacred gift in Hrīṃ sacrifice (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | adhvara:sacrifice | dakṣiṇā:sacred gift); ह्रींकारनन्दनारामनवकल्पकवल्लरी (hrīṃkāranandanārāmanavakalpakavallarī) – wish-fulfilling creeper in Hrīṃ garden (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | nandana:pleasure garden | ārāma:garden | nava:new | kalpaka:wish-fulfilling | vallarī:creeper);

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रींकारहिमवद्गङ्गा ह्रींकारार्णवकौस्तुभा ।
ह्रींकारमन्त्रसर्वस्वा ह्रींकारपरसौख्यदा ॥ ५९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃkārahimavadgaṅgā hrīṃkārārṇavakaustubhā ।
hrīṃkāramantrasarvasvā hrīṃkāraparasaukhyadā ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.translation:
Hrīṃkārahimavadgaṅgā – She who is the sacred Gaṅgā flowing down from the Himalayan height of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkārārṇavakaustubhā – She who is the Kaustubha jewel rising from the ocean of Hrīṃ
Hrīṃkāramantrasarvasvā – She who is the all-in-all, the very life and soul of the Hrīṃ mantra
Hrīṃkāraparasaukhyadā – She who bestows the highest supreme bliss through the Hrīṃ mantra
contentBlocks.meanings: ह्रींकारहिमवद्गङ्गा (hrīṃkārahimavadgaṅgā) – sacred Gaṅgā from Himalayas (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | himavat:Himalayas | gaṅgā:Gaṅgā); ह्रींकारार्णवकौस्तुभा (hrīṃkārārṇavakaustubhā) – Kaustubha jewel from Hrīṃ ocean (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | arṇava:ocean | kaustubhā:Kaustubha jewel); ह्रींकारमन्त्रसर्वस्वा (hrīṃkāramantrasarvasvā) – all-in-all of Hrīṃ mantra (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | mantra:mantra | sarvasvā:all-in-all); ह्रींकारपरसौख्यदा (hrīṃkāraparasaukhyadā) – bestows supreme bliss through Hrīṃ (hrīṃkāra:Hrīṃ sound | para:supreme | saukhyadā:bestows bliss);

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तरपीठिका (फलशृतिः)
हयग्रीव उवाच ।
इत्येवं ते मयाख्यातं देव्या नामशतत्रयम् ।
रहस्यातिरहस्यत्वाद्गोपनीयं त्वया मुने ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttarapīṭhikā (phalaśṛtiḥ)
hayagrīva uvācha ।
ityēvaṃ tē mayākhyātaṃ dēvyā nāmaśatatrayam ।
rahasyātirahasyatvādgōpanīyaṃ tvayā munē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hayagrīva said: Thus I have recited to you the three hundred divine names of the Goddess. Being the most secret of all secrets, this should not be carelessly revealed.
contentBlocks.meanings: उत्तरपीठिका (uttarapīṭhikā) – concluding section (uttara:concluding | pīṭhikā:section); फलशृतिः (phalaśṛtiḥ) – result of hearing (phala:result | śṛtiḥ:hearing); हयग्रीव (hayagrīva) – Hayagrīva; उवाच (uvāca) – said; इति (iti) – thus; एवम् (evam) – in this way; ते (te) – to you; मया (mayā) – by me; आख्यातम् (ākhyātam) – recited; देव्या (devyā) – of the Goddess; नामशतत्रयम् (nāmaśatatrayam) – three hundred names (nāma:names | śata:hundred | trayam:three); रहस्यातिरहस्यत्वात् (rahasyātirahasyatvāt) – most secret of secrets (rahasya:secret | ati:most | rahasyatvāt:of secrets); गोपनीयम् (gopanīyam) – should be concealed; त्वया (tvayā) – by you; मुने (mune) – O sage;

contentBlocks.original:
शिववर्णानि नामानि श्रीदेव्या कथितानि हि ।
शक्त्यक्षराणि नामानि कामेशकथितानि च ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivavarṇāni nāmāni śrīdēvyā kathitāni hi ।
śaktyakṣarāṇi nāmāni kāmēśakathitāni cha ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The names formed by the letters of Śiva (from the Panchadashī mantra) were spoken by Śrī Devī herself; the names formed by the letters of Śakti were spoken by Kāmeśvara.
contentBlocks.meanings: शिववर्णानि (śivavarṇāni) – letters of Śiva (śiva:Śiva | varṇāni:letters); नामानि (nāmāni) – names; श्रीदेव्या (śrīdevyā) – by Śrī Devī (śrī:Śrī | devyā:by Devī); कथितानि (kathitāni) – spoken; हि (hi) – indeed; शक्त्यक्षराणि (śaktyakṣarāṇi) – letters of Śakti (śakti:Śakti | akṣarāṇi:letters); नामानि (nāmāni) – names; कामेशकथितानि (kāmeśakathitāni) – spoken by Kāmeśvara (kāmeśa:Kāmeśvara | kathitāni:spoken); च (ca) – and;

contentBlocks.original:
उभयाक्षरनामानि ह्युभाभ्यां कथितानि वै ।
तदन्यैर्ग्रथितं स्तोत्रमेतस्य सदृशं किमु ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ubhayākṣaranāmāni hyubhābhyāṃ kathitāni vai ।
tadanyairgrathitaṃ stōtramētasya sadṛśaṃ kimu ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The names incorporating both sets of letters were spoken by Śiva and Śakti together. What other hymn composed by anyone else could ever equal this?
contentBlocks.meanings: उभयाक्षरनामानि (ubhayākṣaranāmāni) – names with both letters (ubhaya:both | akṣara:letters | nāmāni:names); हि (hi) – indeed; उभाभ्यां (ubhābhyām) – by both; कथितानि (kathitāni) – spoken; वै (vai) – indeed; तदन्यैः (tadanyaiḥ) – by others (tad:that | anyaiḥ:by others); ग्रथितं (grathitaṃ) – composed; स्तोत्रम् (stotram) – hymn; एतस्य (etasya) – of this; सदृशं (sadṛśaṃ) – equal; किमु (kimu) – what;

contentBlocks.original:
नानेन सदृशं स्तोत्रं श्रीदेवीप्रीतिदायकम् ।
लोकत्रयेऽपि कल्याणं सम्भवेन्नात्र संशयः ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nānēna sadṛśaṃ stōtraṃ śrīdēvīprītidāyakam ।
lōkatrayē'pi kalyāṇaṃ sambhavēnnātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: There is no hymn equal to this one for winning the grace of Śrī Devī. It bestows auspiciousness throughout all three worlds — of this there is no doubt.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; अनेन (anena) – by this; सदृशं (sadṛśam) – equal; स्तोत्रं (stotram) – hymn; श्रीदेवीप्रीतिदायकम् (śrīdevīprītidāyakam) – bestowing Śrī Devī's grace (śrī:Śrī | devī:Devī | prīti:grace | dāyakam:bestowing); लोकत्रयेऽपि (lokatraye'pi) – in all three worlds (loka:world | traye:three | api:also); कल्याणं (kalyāṇam) – auspiciousness; सम्भवेत् (sambhavet) – may occur; न (na) – not; अत्र (atra) – here; संशयः (saṃśayaḥ) – doubt;

contentBlocks.original:
सूत उवाच ।
इति हयमुखगीतं स्तोत्रराजं निशम्य
प्रगलितकलुषोऽभूच्चित्तपर्याप्तिमेत्य ।
निजगुरुमथ नत्वा कुम्भजन्मा तदुक्तं
पुनरधिकरहस्यं ज्ञातुमेवं जगाद ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūta uvācha ।
iti hayamukhagītaṃ stōtrarājaṃ niśamya
pragalitakaluṣō'bhūchchittaparyāptimētya ।
nijagurumatha natvā kumbhajanmā taduktaṃ
punaradhikarahasyaṃ jñātumēvaṃ jagāda ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sūta said: Having heard this king of hymns sung by Hayagrīva, freed from all impurity and his heart filled with contentment, Agastya bowed to his guru with deep devotion.
contentBlocks.meanings: सूत (sūta) – Sūta; उवाच (uvāca) – said; इति (iti) – thus; हयमुखगीतं (hayamukhagītam) – sung by Hayagrīva (haya:horse | mukha:face | gītam:sung); स्तोत्रराजं (stotrarājaṃ) – king of hymns (stotra:hymn | rājaṃ:king); निशम्य (niśamya) – having heard; प्रगलितकलुषः (pragalitakaluṣaḥ) – freed from impurity (pragalita:freed | kaluṣaḥ:impurity); अभूत् (abhūt) – became; चित्तपर्याप्तिम् (cittaparyāptim) – contentment of heart (citta:heart | paryāptim:contentment); एत्य (etya) – having attained; निजगुरुम् (nijagurum) – his own guru (nija:own | gurum:guru); अथ (atha) – then; नत्वा (natvā) – having bowed; कुम्भजन्मा (kumbhajanmā) – Agastya (kumbha:pot | janmā:born); तदुक्तं (taduktaṃ) – that said (tad:that | uktam:said); पुनः (punaḥ) – again; अधिकरहस्यम् (adhikarahasyam) – greater secret (adhika:greater | rahasyam:secret); ज्ञातुम् (jñātum) – to know; एवम् (evam) – thus; जगाद (jagāda) – spoke;

contentBlocks.original:
अगस्त्य उवाच ।
अश्वानन महाभाग रहस्यमपि मे वद ।
शिववर्णानि कान्यत्र शक्तिवर्णानि कानि हि ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
agastya uvācha ।
aśvānana mahābhāga rahasyamapi mē vada ।
śivavarṇāni kānyatra śaktivarṇāni kāni hi ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Agastya said: O great Hayagrīva, please tell me further about this secret — which names belong to the letters of Śiva and which to the letters of Śakti?
contentBlocks.meanings: अगस्त्य (agastya) – Agastya; उवाच (uvāca) – said; अश्वानन (aśvānana) – O horse-faced one (aśva:horse | ānana:face); महाभाग (mahābhāga) – O great one (mahā:great | bhāga:fortunate); रहस्यम् (rahasyam) – secret; अपि (api) – also; मे (me) – to me; वद (vada) – tell; शिववर्णानि (śivavarṇāni) – letters of Śiva (śiva:Śiva | varṇāni:letters); कानि (kāni) – which; अत्र (atra) – here; शक्तिवर्णानि (śaktivarṇāni) – letters of Śakti (śakti:Śakti | varṇāni:letters); कानि (kāni) – which; हि (hi) – indeed;

contentBlocks.original:
उभयोरपि वर्णानि कानि वा वद देशिक ।
इति पृष्टः कुम्भजेन हयग्रीवोऽवदत्पुनः ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ubhayōrapi varṇāni kāni vā vada dēśika ।
iti pṛṣṭaḥ kumbhajēna hayagrīvō'vadatpunaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Which names belong to both? Tell me, O master. Thus questioned by Agastya, Hayagrīva spoke again.
contentBlocks.meanings: उभयोः (ubhayoḥ) – of both; अपि (api) – also; वर्णानि (varṇāni) – names; कानि (kāni) – which; वा (vā) – or; वद (vada) – tell; देशिक (deśika) – O master; इति (iti) – thus; पृष्टः (pṛṣṭaḥ) – questioned; कुम्भजेन (kumbhajena) – by Agastya (kumbha:pot | jena:born from); हयग्रीवः (hayagrīvaḥ) – Hayagrīva; अवदत् (avadat) – spoke; पुनः (punaḥ) – again;

contentBlocks.original:
हयग्रीव उवाच ।
तव गोप्यं किमस्तीह साक्षादम्बानुशासनात् ।
इदं त्वतिरहस्यं ते वक्ष्यामि शृणु कुम्भज ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hayagrīva uvācha ।
tava gōpyaṃ kimastīha sākṣādambānuśāsanāt ।
idaṃ tvatirahasyaṃ tē vakṣyāmi śṛṇu kumbhaja ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Hayagrīva said: What is there that is hidden from you, O Agastya, by direct command of the Mother herself? This most secret of secrets I will tell you — listen, O Kumbhaja.
