contentBlocks.meanings: हयग्रीव (hayagrīva) – Hayagrīva; उवाच (uvāca) – said; तव (tava) – your; गोप्यं (gopyam) – secret; किम् (kim) – what; अस्ति (asti) – is; इह (iha) – here; साक्षात् (sākṣāt) – directly; अम्बा (ambā) – Mother; अनुशासनात् (anuśāsanāt) – by command; इदं (idam) – this; तु (tu) – but; अति (ati) – very; रहस्यम् (rahasyam) – secret; ते (te) – to you; वक्ष्यामि (vakṣyāmi) – I will tell; शृणु (śṛṇu) – listen; कुम्भज (kumbhaja) – O Kumbhaja;

contentBlocks.original:
एतद्विज्ञानमात्रेण श्रीविद्या सिद्धिदा भवेत् ।
कत्रयं हद्वयं चैव शैवो भागः प्रकीर्तितः ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētadvijñānamātrēṇa śrīvidyā siddhidā bhavēt ।
katrayaṃ hadvayaṃ chaiva śaivō bhāgaḥ prakīrtitaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By knowing this alone, the Śrī Vidyā becomes capable of bestowing siddhi. The group of letters Ka, the two letters Ha, and Sa — these constitute the Śaiva portion.
contentBlocks.meanings: एतत् (etat) – this; विज्ञानमात्रेण (vijñānamātreṇa) – by mere knowledge (vijñāna:knowledge | mātreṇa:by mere); श्रीविद्या (śrīvidyā) – Śrī Vidyā (śrī:auspicious | vidyā:knowledge); सिद्धिदा (siddhidā) – bestowing siddhi (siddhi:perfection | dā:giver); भवेत् (bhavet) – becomes; कत्रयम् (katrayam) – group of three Ka (ka:Ka | trayam:group of three); हद्वयम् (hadvayam) – two Ha (ha:Ha | dvayam:pair); च (ca) – and; एव (eva) – indeed; शैवः (śaivaḥ) – Śaiva; भागः (bhāgaḥ) – portion; प्रकीर्तितः (prakīrtitaḥ) – is proclaimed;

contentBlocks.original:
शक्त्यक्षराणि शेषाणि ह्रीङ्कार उभयात्मकः ।
एवं विभागमज्ञात्वा ये विद्याजपशालिनः ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaktyakṣarāṇi śēṣāṇi hrīṅkāra ubhayātmakaḥ ।
ēvaṃ vibhāgamajñātvā yē vidyājapaśālinaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The remaining letters are Śakti letters; the Hrīṃkāra belongs to both. Those who practise japa of the Vidyā without knowing this distribution...
contentBlocks.meanings: शक्त्यक्षराणि (śaktyakṣarāṇi) – Śakti letters (śakti:Śakti | akṣarāṇi:letters); शेषाणि (śeṣāṇi) – remaining; ह्रीङ्कार (hrīṅkāra) – Hrīṃkāra; उभयात्मकः (ubhayātmakaḥ) – belongs to both; एवं (evaṃ) – thus; विभागम् (vibhāgam) – distribution; अज्ञात्वा (ajñātvā) – without knowing; ये (ye) – those who; विद्याजपशालिनः (vidyājapaśālinaḥ) – practise japa of the Vidyā (vidyā:Vidyā | japa:japa | śālinaḥ:practise);

contentBlocks.original:
न तेषां सिद्धिदा विद्या कल्पकोटिशतैरपि ।
चतुर्भिः शिवचक्रैश्च शक्तिचक्रैश्च पञ्चभिः ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na tēṣāṃ siddhidā vidyā kalpakōṭiśatairapi ।
chaturbhiḥ śivachakraiścha śaktichakraiścha pañchabhiḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...will not attain siddhi through the Vidyā even in hundreds of crores of kalpas. The Śrī Chakra has four Śaiva chakras and five Śakti chakras...
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; तेषाम् (teṣām) – of them; सिद्धिदा (siddhidā) – giver of success (siddhi:success | dā:giver); विद्या (vidyā) – knowledge; कल्पकोटिशतैः (kalpakoṭiśataiḥ) – by hundreds of crores of kalpas (kalpa:kalpa | koṭi:crore | śataiḥ:by hundreds); अपि (api) – even; चतुर्भिः (caturbhiḥ) – by four; शिवचक्रैः (śivachakraiḥ) – by Śaiva chakras (śiva:Śaiva | chakraiḥ:by chakras); च (ca) – and; शक्तिचक्रैः (śaktichakraiḥ) – by Śakti chakras (śakti:Śakti | chakraiḥ:by chakras); च (ca) – and; पञ्चभिः (pañcabhiḥ) – by five;

contentBlocks.original:
नवचक्रैश्च संसिद्धं श्रीचक्रं शिवयोर्वपुः ।
त्रिकोणमष्टकोणं च दशकोणद्वयं तथा ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
navachakraiścha saṃsiddhaṃ śrīchakraṃ śivayōrvapuḥ ।
trikōṇamaṣṭakōṇaṃ cha daśakōṇadvayaṃ tathā ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...nine chakras in all, constituting the combined form of Śiva and Śakti. The triangle, the octagon, the two decagons...
contentBlocks.meanings: नवचक्रैः (navachakraiḥ) – with nine chakras (nava:nine | chakraiḥ:with chakras); च (ca) – and; संसिद्धं (saṃsiddhaṃ) – perfected; श्रीचक्रं (śrīcakraṃ) – Śrī Chakra (śrī:auspicious | cakraṃ:chakra); शिवयोः (śivayoḥ) – of Śiva and Śakti (śiva:Śiva | yoḥ:of the two); वपुः (vapuḥ) – form; त्रिकोणम् (trikoṇam) – triangle; अष्टकोणं (aṣṭakoṇaṃ) – octagon; च (ca) – and; दशकोणद्वयं (daśakoṇadvayaṃ) – two decagons (daśakoṇa:decagon | dvayaṃ:two); तथा (tathā) – thus;

contentBlocks.original:
चतुर्दशारं चैतानि शक्तिचक्राणि पञ्च च ।
बिन्दुश्चाष्टदलं पद्मं पद्मं षोडशपत्रकम् ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturdaśāraṃ chaitāni śaktichakrāṇi pañcha cha ।
binduśchāṣṭadalaṃ padmaṃ padmaṃ ṣōḍaśapatrakam ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...and the fourteen-petalled figure — these are the five Śakti chakras. The bindu, the eight-petalled lotus, the sixteen-petalled lotus...
contentBlocks.meanings: चतुर्दशारं (chaturdaśāram) – fourteen-petalled (chatur:four | daśāram:petalled); च (ca) – and; एतानि (etāni) – these; शक्तिचक्राणि (śaktichakrāṇi) – Śakti chakras (śakti:Śakti | chakrāṇi:chakras); पञ्च (pañca) – five; च (ca) – and; बिन्दुः (binduḥ) – bindu; च (ca) – and; अष्टदलं (aṣṭadalaṃ) – eight-petalled (aṣṭa:eight | dalaṃ:petalled); पद्मं (padmaṃ) – lotus; पद्मं (padmaṃ) – lotus; षोडशपत्रकम् (ṣoḍaśapatrakam) – sixteen-petalled (ṣoḍaśa:sixteen | patrakam:petalled);

contentBlocks.original:
चतुरश्रं च चत्वारि शिवचक्राण्यनुक्रमात् ।
त्रिकोणे बैन्दवं श्लिष्टं अष्टारेऽष्टदलाम्बुजम् ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaturaśraṃ cha chatvāri śivachakrāṇyanukramāt ।
trikōṇē baindavaṃ śliṣṭaṃ aṣṭārē'ṣṭadalāmbujam ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...and the square enclosure — these four in order are the Śaiva chakras. The triangle is joined with the bindu, the eight-petalled lotus with the eight-pointed star...
contentBlocks.meanings: चतुरश्रं (chaturaśram) – square enclosure (chatur:four | aśram:enclosure); च (ca) – and; चत्वारि (chatvāri) – four; शिवचक्राणि (śivachakrāṇi) – Śaiva chakras (śiva:Śaiva | chakrāṇi:chakras); अनुक्रमात् (anukramāt) – in order; त्रिकोणे (trikoṇe) – in the triangle (tri:three | koṇe:in the angle); बैन्दवं (baindavam) – with the bindu; श्लिष्टं (śliṣṭam) – joined; अष्टारे (aṣṭāre) – eight-pointed star (aṣṭa:eight | āre:pointed star); अष्टदलाम्बुजम् (aṣṭadalāmbujam) – eight-petalled lotus (aṣṭa:eight | dala:petalled | ambujam:lotus);

contentBlocks.original:
दशारयोः षोडशारं भूगृहं भुवनाश्रके ।
शैवानामपि शाक्तानां चक्राणां च परस्परम् ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśārayōḥ ṣōḍaśāraṃ bhūgṛhaṃ bhuvanāśrakē ।
śaivānāmapi śāktānāṃ chakrāṇāṃ cha parasparam ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...the two ten-petalled figures with the sixteen-petalled one, and the outer square with the outermost enclosure. There is an inseparable relationship between the Śaiva and Śākta chakras...
contentBlocks.meanings: दशारयोः (daśārayoḥ) – of the two ten-petalled (daśa:ten | ārayoḥ:of the two petalled); षोडशारं (ṣoḍaśāraṃ) – sixteen-petalled (ṣoḍaśa:sixteen | āraṃ:petalled); भूगृहं (bhūgṛhaṃ) – earth house (bhū:earth | gṛhaṃ:house); भुवनाश्रके (bhuvanāśrake) – in the outermost enclosure (bhuvana:world | āśrake:in the enclosure); शैवानाम् (śaivānām) – of the Śaivas; अपि (api) – also; शाक्तानां (śāktānāṃ) – of the Śāktas; चक्राणां (chakrāṇāṃ) – of the chakras; च (ca) – and; परस्परम् (parasparam) – mutual;

contentBlocks.original:
अविनाभावसम्बन्धं यो जानाति स चक्रवित् ।
त्रिकोणरूपिणी शक्तिर्बिन्दुरूपपरः शिवः ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avinābhāvasambandhaṃ yō jānāti sa chakravit ।
trikōṇarūpiṇī śaktirbindurūpaparaḥ śivaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...and one who knows this inseparable relationship is truly a knower of the Śrī Chakra. Śakti has the form of the triangle; Śiva in the form of the bindu is supreme.
contentBlocks.meanings: अविनाभावसम्बन्धं (avinābhāvasambandhaṃ) – inseparable relationship (avinābhāva:inseparable | sambandhaṃ:relationship); यः (yaḥ) – who; जानाति (jānāti) – knows; स (sa) – he; चक्रवित् (chakravit) – knower of the Śrī Chakra (chakra:Śrī Chakra | vit:knower); त्रिकोणरूपिणी (trikoṇarūpiṇī) – having the form of a triangle (trikoṇa:triangle | rūpiṇī:having the form); शक्तिः (śaktiḥ) – Śakti; बिन्दुरूपपरः (bindurūpaparaḥ) – supreme in the form of bindu (bindu:bindu | rūpa:form | paraḥ:supreme); शिवः (śivaḥ) – Śiva;

contentBlocks.original:
अविनाभावसम्बन्धं तस्माद्बिन्दुत्रिकोणयोः ।
एवं विभागमज्ञात्वा श्रीचक्रं यः समर्चयेत् ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
avinābhāvasambandhaṃ tasmādbindutrikōṇayōḥ ।
ēvaṃ vibhāgamajñātvā śrīchakraṃ yaḥ samarchayēt ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Therefore there is an inseparable relationship between the bindu and the triangle. One who worships the Śrī Chakra without knowing this internal division...
contentBlocks.meanings: अविनाभावसम्बन्धं (avinābhāvasambandhaṃ) – inseparable relationship (avinābhāva:inseparable | sambandhaṃ:relationship); तस्मात् (tasmāt) – therefore; बिन्दुत्रिकोणयोः (bindutrikōṇayoḥ) – of the bindu and triangle (bindu:bindu | trikōṇa:triangle | yoḥ:of the); एवं (evaṃ) – thus; विभागम् (vibhāgam) – division; अज्ञात्वा (ajñātvā) – without knowing; श्रीचक्रं (śrīcakraṃ) – Śrī Chakra (śrī:holy | cakraṃ:wheel); यः (yaḥ) – who; समर्चयेत् (samarchayet) – worships;

contentBlocks.original:
न तत्फलमवाप्नोति ललिताम्बा न तुष्यति ।
ये च जानन्ति लोकेऽस्मिन् श्रीविद्याचक्रवेदिनः ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na tatphalamavāpnōti lalitāmbā na tuṣyati ।
yē cha jānanti lōkē'smin śrīvidyāchakravēdinaḥ ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...does not obtain its fruit, and the Goddess Lalitāmbā is not pleased. Those in this world who know the Śrī Vidyā and the Śrī Chakra...
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; तत्फलम् (tatphalam) – its fruit (tat:its | phalam:fruit); अवाप्नोति (avāpnoti) – obtains; ललिताम्बा (lalitāmbā) – Goddess Lalitāmbā (lalitā:Lalitā | ambā:mother/goddess); न (na) – not; तुष्यति (tuṣyati) – is pleased; ये (ye) – those who; च (ca) – and; जानन्ति (jānanti) – know; लोके (loke) – in the world; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; श्रीविद्याचक्रवेदिनः (śrīvidyācakravedinaḥ) – knowers of Śrī Vidyā and Śrī Chakra (śrīvidyā:Śrī Vidyā | cakra:Śrī Chakra | vedinaḥ:knowers);

contentBlocks.original:
सामन्यवेदिनः सर्वे विशेषज्ञोऽतिदुर्लभः ।
स्वयंविद्याविशेषज्ञो विशेषज्ञं समर्चयेत् ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sāmanyavēdinaḥ sarvē viśēṣajñō'tidurlabhaḥ ।
svayaṃvidyāviśēṣajñō viśēṣajñaṃ samarchayēt ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...all know it only at the general level; one who knows the specific and complete understanding is extremely rare.
contentBlocks.meanings: सामन्यवेदिनः (sāmanyavedinaḥ) – general knowers (sāmanya:general | vedinaḥ:knowers); सर्वे (sarve) – all; विशेषज्ञः (viśeṣajñaḥ) – specific knower (viśeṣa:specific | jñaḥ:knower); अतिदुर्लभः (atidurlabhaḥ) – extremely rare (ati:extremely | durlabhaḥ:rare); स्वयंविद्याविशेषज्ञः (svayaṃvidyāviśeṣajñaḥ) – self-knowledge specific knower (svayaṃ:self | vidyā:knowledge | viśeṣajñaḥ:specific knower); विशेषज्ञं (viśeṣajñaṃ) – specific knower (viśeṣa:specific | jñaṃ:knower); समर्चयेत् (samarchayet) – should honor;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्मै देयं ततो ग्राह्यमशक्तस्तस्य दापयेत् ।
अन्धं तमः प्रविशन्ति येऽविद्यां समुपासते ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmai dēyaṃ tatō grāhyamaśaktastasya dāpayēt ।
andhaṃ tamaḥ praviśanti yē'vidyāṃ samupāsatē ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who has specific knowledge should worship another knower of the Vidyā and receive instruction from them. 'They enter blind darkness who worship only Avidyā' — thus says the Śruti.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्मै (tasmai) – to him; देयम् (deyam) – should be given; ततः (tataḥ) – then; ग्राह्यम् (grāhyam) – should be received; अशक्तः (aśaktaḥ) – unable; तस्य (tasya) – his; दापयेत् (dāpayet) – should cause to give; अन्धम् (andham) – blind; तमः (tamaḥ) – darkness; प्रविशन्ति (praviśanti) – enter; ये (ye) – who; अविद्याम् (avidyām) – ignorance; समुपासते (samupāsate) – worship;

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रुतिरपाहैतानविद्योपासकान्पुनः ।
विद्यान्योपासकानेव निन्दत्यारुणिकी श्रुतिः ॥ २१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrutirapāhaitānavidyōpāsakānpunaḥ ।
vidyānyōpāsakānēva nindatyāruṇikī śrutiḥ ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus the Śruti declares, and the Āruṇī Śruti also condemns those who take pride in mere learning while neglecting the true Vidyā.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; श्रुतिः (śrutiḥ) – the Śruti; अपाह (apāha) – declares; एतान् (etān) – these; अविद्या (avidyā) – ignorance; उपासकान् (upāsakān) – worshippers; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; विद्या (vidyā) – knowledge; अन्य (anya) – other; उपासकान् (upāsakān) – worshippers; एव (eva) – indeed; निन्दति (nindati) – condemns; आरुणिकी (āruṇikī) – Āruṇī; श्रुतिः (śrutiḥ) – the Śruti;

contentBlocks.original:
अश्रुता सश्रुतासश्च यज्वानो येऽप्ययज्वनः ।
स्वर्यन्तो नापेक्षन्ते इन्द्रमग्निं च ये विदुः ॥ २२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aśrutā saśrutāsaścha yajvānō yē'pyayajvanaḥ ।
svaryantō nāpēkṣantē indramagniṃ cha yē viduḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Those who have heard and those who have not, those who have sacrificed and those who have not — those who know Indra and Agni transcend even these gods.
contentBlocks.meanings: अश्रुताः (aśrutāḥ) – those who have not heard; सश्रुताः (saśrutāḥ) – those who have heard; अश्च (aśca) – and those who; यज्वानः (yajvānaḥ) – those who have sacrificed; ये (ye) – those who; अपि (api) – also; अयज्वनः (ayajvanaḥ) – those who have not sacrificed; स्वर्यन्तः (svaryantaḥ) – transcend; न (na) – not; अपेक्षन्ते (apekṣante) – depend on; इन्द्रम् (indram) – Indra; अग्निम् (agnim) – Agni; च (ca) – and; ये (ye) – those who; विदुः (viduḥ) – know;

contentBlocks.original:
सिकता इव संयन्ति रश्मिभिः समुदीरिताः ।
अस्माल्लोकादमुष्माच्चेत्याह चारण्यकश्रुतिः ॥ २३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sikatā iva saṃyanti raśmibhiḥ samudīritāḥ ।
asmāllōkādamuṣmāchchētyāha chāraṇyakaśrutiḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: They dissolve like sand stirred by sunrays. Thus says the Āraṇyaka text: they depart from this world and that world...
contentBlocks.meanings: सिकता (sikatā) – sand; इव (iva) – like; संयन्ति (saṃyanti) – dissolve; रश्मिभिः (raśmibhiḥ) – by sunrays; समुदीरिताः (samudīritāḥ) – stirred; अस्मात् (asmāt) – from this; लोकात् (lokāt) – world; अमुष्मात् (amuṣmāt) – from that; च (ca) – and; इति (iti) – thus; आह (āha) – says; चारण्यकश्रुतिः (cāraṇyakaśrutiḥ) – Āraṇyaka text (cāraṇyaka:Āraṇyaka | śrutiḥ:text);

contentBlocks.original:
यस्य नो पश्चिमं जन्म यदि वा शङ्करः स्वयम् ।
तेनैव लभ्यते विद्या श्रीमत्पञ्चदशाक्षरी ॥ २४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasya nō paśchimaṃ janma yadi vā śaṅkaraḥ svayam ।
tēnaiva labhyatē vidyā śrīmatpañchadaśākṣarī ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One whose final birth this is, or one whom Śaṅkara himself wills to bless — by such a one alone is the Śrī Pañcadaśī Vidyā attained.
contentBlocks.meanings: यस्य (yasya) – whose; नः (naḥ) – our; पश्चिमं (paścimaṃ) – final; जन्म (janma) – birth; यदि (yadi) – if; वा (vā) – or; शङ्करः (śaṅkaraḥ) – Śaṅkara; स्वयम् (svayam) – himself; तेनैव (tenaiva) – by such a one alone (tena:by him | eva:alone); लभ्यते (labhyate) – is attained; विद्या (vidyā) – knowledge; श्रीमत्पञ्चदशाक्षरी (śrīmatpañcadaśākṣarī) – Śrī Pañcadaśī Vidyā (śrīmat:glorious | pañcadaśa:fifteen | akṣarī:syllables);

contentBlocks.original:
इति मन्त्रेषु बहुधा विद्याया महिमोच्यते ।
मोक्षैकहेतुविद्या तु श्रीविद्या नात्र संशयः ॥ २५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti mantrēṣu bahudhā vidyāyā mahimōchyatē ।
mōkṣaikahētuvidyā tu śrīvidyā nātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus the greatness of the Vidyā is proclaimed in many ways throughout the mantras. The Vidyā that is the sole cause of liberation is the Śrī Vidyā — of this there is no doubt.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; मन्त्रेषु (mantreṣu) – in the mantras; बहुधा (bahudhā) – in many ways; विद्यायाः (vidyāyāḥ) – of the Vidyā; महिमा (mahimā) – greatness; उच्यते (ucyate) – is proclaimed; मोक्षैकहेतुविद्या (mokṣaikahetuvidyā) – Vidyā, sole cause of liberation (mokṣa:liberation | eka:sole | hetu:cause | vidyā:Vidyā); तु (tu) – but; श्रीविद्या (śrīvidyā) – Śrī Vidyā (śrī:Śrī | vidyā:Vidyā); न (na) – no; अत्र (atra) – here; संशयः (saṃśayaḥ) – doubt;

contentBlocks.original:
न शिल्पादिज्ञानयुक्ते विद्वच्छब्धः प्रयुज्यते ।
मोक्षैकहेतुविद्या सा श्रीविद्यैव न संशयः ॥ २६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na śilpādijñānayuktē vidvachChabdhaḥ prayujyatē ।
mōkṣaikahētuvidyā sā śrīvidyaiva na saṃśayaḥ ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The word 'vidvān' (the learned one) is not properly applied to those learned only in arts and crafts. The Vidyā that leads to liberation is the Śrī Vidyā alone — of this there is no doubt.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; शिल्पादिज्ञानयुक्ते (śilpādijñānayukte) – endowed with arts knowledge (śilpa:arts | ādi:and others | jñāna:knowledge | yukte:endowed with); विद्वच्छब्धः (vidvachChabdaḥ) – word 'vidvān' (vidvat:learned | śabdaḥ:word); प्रयुज्यते (prayujyate) – is applied; मोक्षैकहेतुविद्या (mokṣaikahetuvidyā) – knowledge leading to liberation (mokṣa:liberation | eka:one | hetu:cause | vidyā:knowledge); सा (sā) – that; श्रीविद्या (śrīvidyā) – Śrī Vidyā (śrī:auspicious | vidyā:knowledge); एव (eva) – indeed; न (na) – no; संशयः (saṃśayaḥ) – doubt;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्माद्विद्याविदेवात्र विद्वान्विद्वानितीर्यते ।
स्वयं विद्याविदे दद्यात्ख्यापयेत्तद्गुणान्सुधीः ॥ २७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasmādvidyāvidēvātra vidvānvidvānitīryatē ।
svayaṃ vidyāvidē dadyātkhyāpayēttadguṇānsudhīḥ ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Therefore, one who knows the Vidyā is truly called a 'vidvān' here. An intelligent person should give to a knower of the Vidyā and proclaim that person's virtues.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्मात् (tasmāt) – therefore; विद्याविदा (vidyāvidā) – knower of the Vidyā (vidyā:knowledge | vidā:knower); एव (eva) – indeed; अत्र (atra) – here; विद्वान् (vidvān) – wise person; विद्वान् (vidvān) – wise person; इति (iti) – thus; ईर्यते (īryate) – is called; स्वयम् (svayam) – oneself; विद्याविदे (vidyāvide) – to the knower of Vidyā (vidyā:knowledge | vide:to the knower); दद्यात् (dadyāt) – should give; ख्यापयेत् (khyāpayet) – should proclaim; तत् (tat) – that; गुणान् (guṇān) – virtues; सुधीः (sudhīḥ) – intelligent person;

contentBlocks.original:
स्वयंविद्यारहस्यज्ञो विद्यामाहात्म्यवेद्यपि ।
विद्याविदं नार्चयेच्चेत्को वा तं पूजयेज्जनः ॥ २८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svayaṃvidyārahasyajñō vidyāmāhātmyavēdyapi ।
vidyāvidaṃ nārchayēchchētkō vā taṃ pūjayējjanaḥ ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who knows the Vidyā's secret and its greatness, yet does not worship a knower of the Vidyā — who among people would revere such a one?
contentBlocks.meanings: स्वयं (svayam) – oneself; विद्या (vidyā) – knowledge; रहस्यज्ञः (rahasyajñaḥ) – knower of secrets (rahasya:secret | jñaḥ:knower); विद्यामाहात्म्यवेद्यपि (vidyāmāhātmyavedya) – knower of Vidyā's greatness (vidyā:knowledge | māhātmya:greatness | vedya:knower); अपि (api) – even; विद्याविदम् (vidyāvidam) – knower of knowledge (vidyā:knowledge | vidam:knower); न (na) – not; अर्चयेत् (arcayet) – worships; चेत् (cet) – if; कः (kaḥ) – who; वा (vā) – or; तम् (tam) – him; पूजयेत् (pūjayet) – would revere; जनः (janaḥ) – people;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रसङ्गादिदमुक्तं ते प्रकृतं शृणु कुम्भज ।
यः कीर्तयेत्सकृद्भक्त्या दिव्यनामशतत्रयम् ॥ २९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prasaṅgādidamuktaṃ tē prakṛtaṃ śṛṇu kumbhaja ।
yaḥ kīrtayētsakṛdbhaktyā divyanāmaśatatrayam ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This has been said by way of context. Now listen to what is relevant, O Kumbhaja. Whoever recites with devotion even once these three hundred divine names...
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रसङ्गात् (prasaṅgāt) – by way of context; इदम् (idam) – this; उक्तं (uktaṃ) – said; ते (te) – by you; प्रकृतं (prakṛtaṃ) – relevant; शृणु (śṛṇu) – listen; कुम्भज (kumbhaja) – O Kumbhaja; यः (yaḥ) – whoever; कीर्तयेत् (kīrtayet) – recites; सकृत् (sakṛt) – even once; भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; दिव्यनामशतत्रयम् (divyanāmaśatatrayam) – three hundred divine names (divya:divine | nāma:names | śata:hundred | trayam:three);

contentBlocks.original:
तस्य पुण्यमहं वक्ष्ये शृणु त्वं कुम्भसम्भव ।
रहस्यनामसाहस्रपाठे यत्फलमीरितम् ॥ ३० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasya puṇyamahaṃ vakṣyē śṛṇu tvaṃ kumbhasambhava ।
rahasyanāmasāhasrapāṭhē yatphalamīritam ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...I will tell you the merit accruing to that person — listen, O Kumbhasambhava. The fruit declared for reciting the Rahasya Nāmasāhasra (the secret Thousand Names)...
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्य (tasya) – of that; पुण्यम् (puṇyam) – merit; अहम् (aham) – I; वक्ष्ये (vakṣye) – will tell; शृणु (śṛṇu) – listen; त्वम् (tvam) – you; कुम्भसम्भव (kumbhasambhava) – O Kumbhasambhava (kumbha:pot | sambhava:born of); रहस्यनामसाहस्रपाठे (rahasyanāmasāhasrapāṭhe) – in reciting the secret Thousand Names (rahasya:secret | nāma:name | sāhasra:thousand | pāṭhe:in reciting); यत् (yat) – which; फलम् (phalam) – fruit; ईरितम् (īritam) – declared;

contentBlocks.original:
तत्फलं कोटिगुणितमेकनामजपाद्भवेत् ।
कामेश्वरीकामेशाभ्यां कृतं नामशतत्रयम् ॥ ३१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatphalaṃ kōṭiguṇitamēkanāmajapādbhavēt ।
kāmēśvarīkāmēśābhyāṃ kṛtaṃ nāmaśatatrayam ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...that same fruit, multiplied a crore-fold, accrues from the japa of even a single name here. These three hundred names were composed by Kāmeśvarī and Kāmeśvara together.
contentBlocks.meanings: तत्फलम् (tatphalam) – that fruit (tat:that | phalam:fruit); कोटिगुणितम् (koṭiguṇitam) – multiplied a crore-fold (koṭi:crore | guṇitam:multiplied); एकनामजपात् (ekanāmajapāt) – from the japa of one name (eka:one | nāma:name | japāt:from the japa); भवेत् (bhavet) – accrues; कामेश्वरीकामेशाभ्याम् (kāmeśvarīkāmeśābhyām) – by Kāmeśvarī and Kāmeśvara (kāmeśvarī:Kāmeśvarī | kāmeśābhyām:by Kāmeśvara); कृतम् (kṛtam) – composed; नामशतत्रयम् (nāmaśatatrayam) – three hundred names (nāma:names | śata:hundred | trayam:three);

contentBlocks.original:
नान्येन तुलयेदेतत् स्तोत्रेणान्यकृतेन च ।
श्रियः परम्परा यस्य भावि वा चोत्तरोत्तरम् ॥ ३२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nānyēna tulayēdētat stōtrēṇānyakṛtēna cha ।
śriyaḥ paramparā yasya bhāvi vā chōttarōttaram ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One should not compare this stotra with any other hymn composed by another. One whose prosperity continues to grow generation after generation...
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; अन्येन (anyena) – by another; तुलयेत् (tulayet) – should compare; एतत् (etat) – this; स्तोत्रेण (stotreṇa) – with hymn; अन्यकृतेन (anyakṛtena) – composed by another (anya:another | kṛtena:composed by); च (ca) – and; श्रियः (śriyaḥ) – prosperity; परम्परा (paramparā) – generation after generation; यस्य (yasya) – whose; भावि (bhāvi) – future; वा (vā) – or; च (ca) – and; उत्तरः (uttaraḥ) – further; उत्तरम् (uttaram) – more;

contentBlocks.original:
तेनैव लभ्यते चैतत्पश्चाच्छ्रेयः परीक्षयेत् ।
अस्या नाम्नां त्रिशत्यास्तु महिमा केन वर्ण्यते ॥ ३३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tēnaiva labhyatē chaitatpaśchāchChrēyaḥ parīkṣayēt ।
asyā nāmnāṃ triśatyāstu mahimā kēna varṇyatē ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...by that person alone can this be attained, and thereafter they shall examine the reward. Who can describe the greatness of these three hundred names of Hers?
contentBlocks.meanings: तेनैव (tenaiva) – by that alone (tena:by that | eva:alone); लभ्यते (labhyate) – is attained; च (ca) – and; एतत् (etat) – this; पश्चात् (paścāt) – thereafter; श्रेयः (śreyaḥ) – reward; परीक्षयेत् (parīkṣayet) – should examine; अस्या (asyā) – of Hers; नाम्नाम् (nāmnām) – of names; त्रिशत्याः (triśatyāḥ) – three hundred; तु (tu) – but; महिमा (mahimā) – greatness; केन (kena) – by whom; वर्ण्यते (varṇyate) – is described;

contentBlocks.original:
या स्वयं शिवयोर्वक्त्रपद्माभ्यां परिनिःसृता ।
नित्यं षोडशसङ्ख्याकान्विप्रानादौ तु भोजयेत् ॥ ३४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yā svayaṃ śivayōrvaktrapadmābhyāṃ pariniḥsṛtā ।
nityaṃ ṣōḍaśasaṅkhyākānviprānādau tu bhōjayēt ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This stotra, which emerged from the lotus-mouths of Śiva and Śakti themselves — first, sixteen brāhmaṇas who are knowers of the Vedas should be fed.
contentBlocks.meanings: या (yā) – which; स्वयं (svayaṃ) – by itself; शिवयोः (śivayoḥ) – of Śiva and Śakti (śiva:Śiva | yoḥ:of the two); वक्त्रपद्माभ्यां (vaktrapadmābhyāṃ) – from the lotus-mouths (vaktra:mouth | padma:lotus | abhyāṃ:from the two); परिनिःसृता (pariniḥsṛtā) – emerged; नित्यं (nityaṃ) – always; षोडशसङ्ख्याकान् (ṣoḍaśasaṅkhyākān) – sixteen in number (ṣoḍaśa:sixteen | saṅkhyākān:in number); विप्रान् (viprān) – brāhmaṇas; आदौ (ādau) – first; तु (tu) – but; भोजयेत् (bhojayet) – should be fed;

contentBlocks.original:
अभ्यक्तांस्तिलतैलेन स्नातानुष्णेन वारिणा ।
अभ्यर्च्य गन्धपुष्पाद्यैः कामेश्वर्यादिनामभिः ॥ ३५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abhyaktāṃstilatailēna snātānuṣṇēna vāriṇā ।
abhyarchya gandhapuṣpādyaiḥ kāmēśvaryādināmabhiḥ ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having anointed them with sesame oil and bathed them with warm water, having worshipped them with sandal paste, flowers, and other offerings, reciting the names of Kāmeśvarī and others...
contentBlocks.meanings: अभ्यक्तान् (abhyaktān) – anointed; तिलतैलेन (tilatailena) – with sesame oil (tila:sesame | tailena:with oil); स्नातान् (snātān) – bathed; उष्णेन (uṣṇena) – with warm; वारिणा (vāriṇā) – with water; अभ्यर्च्य (abhyarcya) – having worshipped; गन्धपुष्पाद्यैः (gandhapuṣpādyaiḥ) – with sandal paste, flowers, etc. (gandha:sandal paste | puṣpa:flowers | ādyaiḥ:and others); कामेश्वर्यादिनामभिः (kāmeśvaryādināmabhiḥ) – reciting names of Kāmeśvarī, etc. (kāmeśvarī:Kāmeśvarī | ādi:and others | nāmabhiḥ:with names);

contentBlocks.original:
सूपापूपैः शर्कराद्यैः पायसैः फलसंयुतैः ।
विद्याविदो विशेषेण भोजयेत्षोडश द्विजान् ॥ ३६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūpāpūpaiḥ śarkarādyaiḥ pāyasaiḥ phalasaṃyutaiḥ ।
vidyāvidō viśēṣēṇa bhōjayētṣōḍaśa dvijān ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...with curries, sweets, sugar preparations, rice pudding, and fruits — especially brāhmaṇas who are knowers of the Vidyā should be fed — sixteen in number.
contentBlocks.meanings: सूपापूपैः (sūpāpūpaiḥ) – with curries and cakes (sūpa:curry | āpūpaiḥ:with cakes); शर्कराद्यैः (śarkarādyaiḥ) – with sugar preparations (śarkarā:sugar | ādyaiḥ:and others); पायसैः (pāyasaiḥ) – with rice pudding; फलसंयुतैः (phalasaṃyutaiḥ) – with fruits (phala:fruits | saṃyutaiḥ:with); विद्याविदः (vidyāvidaḥ) – knowers of Vidyā (vidyā:knowledge | vidaḥ:knowers); विशेषेण (viśeṣeṇa) – especially; भोजयेत् (bhojayet) – should be fed; षोडश (ṣoḍaśa) – sixteen; द्विजान् (dvijān) – brāhmaṇas;

contentBlocks.original:
एवं नित्यार्चनं कुर्यादादौ ब्राह्मणभोजनम् ।
त्रिशतीनामभिः पश्चाद्ब्राह्मणान्क्रमशोऽर्चयेत् ॥ ३७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ēvaṃ nityārchanaṃ kuryādādau brāhmaṇabhōjanam ।
triśatīnāmabhiḥ paśchādbrāhmaṇānkramaśō'rchayēt ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One should thus perform daily worship, and first offer food to brāhmaṇas. Thereafter, one should honour the brāhmaṇas in order with the Trishatī names.
contentBlocks.meanings: एवम् (evam) – thus; नित्य (nitya) – daily; अर्चनं (arcanam) – worship; कुर्यात् (kuryāt) – should perform; आदौ (ādau) – first; ब्राह्मणभोजनम् (brāhmaṇabhojanam) – offering food to brāhmaṇas (brāhmaṇa:brāhmaṇas | bhojanam:food offering); त्रिशतीनामभिः (triśatīnāmabhiḥ) – with the Trishatī names (triśatī:Trishatī | nāmabhiḥ:with names); पश्चात् (paścāt) – thereafter; ब्राह्मणान् (brāhmaṇān) – brāhmaṇas; क्रमशः (kramaśaḥ) – in order; अर्चयेत् (arcayet) – should honour;

contentBlocks.original:
तैलाभ्यङ्गादिकं दत्वा विभवे सति भक्तितः ।
शुक्लप्रतिपदारभ्य पौर्णमास्यवधि क्रमात् ॥ ३८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tailābhyaṅgādikaṃ datvā vibhavē sati bhaktitaḥ ।
śuklapratipadārabhya paurṇamāsyavadhi kramāt ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having given them oil massage and other gifts according to one's means and with devotion, beginning from the first day of the bright fortnight up to the full moon day...
contentBlocks.meanings: तैलाभ्यङ्गादिकं (tailābhyaṅgādikaṃ) – oil massage and others (taila:oil | abhyaṅga:massage | ādikaṃ:and others); दत्वा (datvā) – having given; विभवे (vibhave) – according to means; सति (sati) – being; भक्तितः (bhaktitaḥ) – with devotion; शुक्लप्रतिपदारभ्य (śuklapratipadārabhya) – beginning from the first day (śukla:bright | pratipadā:first day | ārabhya:beginning from); पौर्णमास्यवधि (paurṇamāsyavadhi) – up to the full moon day (paurṇamāsī:full moon day | avadhi:up to); क्रमात् (kramāt) – in sequence;

contentBlocks.original:
दिवसे दिवसे विप्रा भोज्या विंशतिसङ्ख्यया ।
दशभिः पञ्चभिर्वापि त्रिभिरेकेन वा दिनैः ॥ ३९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
divasē divasē viprā bhōjyā viṃśatisaṅkhyayā ।
daśabhiḥ pañchabhirvāpi tribhirēkēna vā dinaiḥ ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...day by day, twenty brāhmaṇas should be fed. Or with ten, or five, or three, or even one per day...
contentBlocks.meanings: दिवसे (divase) – day by day; दिवसे (divase) – day by day; विप्राः (viprāḥ) – brāhmaṇas; भोज्याः (bhojyāḥ) – should be fed; विंशतिसङ्ख्यया (viṃśatisaṅkhyayā) – with twenty in number (viṃśati:twenty | saṅkhyayā:in number); दशभिः (daśabhiḥ) – with ten; पञ्चभिः (pañcabhiḥ) – with five; वा (vā) – or; अपि (api) – even; त्रिभिः (tribhiḥ) – with three; एकेन (ekena) – with one; वा (vā) – or; दिनैः (dinaiḥ) – per day;

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिंशत्षष्टिः शतं विप्राः सम्भोज्यास्त्रिशतं क्रमात् ।
एवं यः कुरुते भक्त्या जन्ममध्ये सकृन्नरः ॥ ४० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
triṃśatṣaṣṭiḥ śataṃ viprāḥ sambhōjyāstriśataṃ kramāt ।
ēvaṃ yaḥ kurutē bhaktyā janmamadhyē sakṛnnaraḥ ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...thirty, sixty, a hundred, or three hundred brāhmaṇas in total should be fed. One who does this even once in a lifetime with devotion...
contentBlocks.meanings: त्रिंशत्षष्टिः (triṃśatṣaṣṭiḥ) – thirty and sixty (triṃśat:thirty | ṣaṣṭiḥ:sixty); शतं (śataṃ) – a hundred; विप्राः (viprāḥ) – brāhmaṇas; सम्भोज्याः (sambhojyāḥ) – should be fed; त्रिशतं (triśataṃ) – three hundred; क्रमात् (kramāt) – in total; एवं (evaṃ) – thus; यः (yaḥ) – who; कुरुते (kurute) – does; भक्त्या (bhaktyā) – with devotion; जन्ममध्ये (janmamadhye) – in a lifetime (janma:birth | madhye:in the middle); सकृत् (sakṛt) – once; नरः (naraḥ) – man;

contentBlocks.original:
तस्यैव सफलं जन्म मुक्तिस्तस्य करे स्थिरा ।
रहस्यनामसाहस्रभोजनेऽप्येवमेव हि ॥ ४१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tasyaiva saphalaṃ janma muktistasya karē sthirā ।
rahasyanāmasāhasrabhōjanē'pyēvamēva hi ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...for that person alone is this birth fruitful; liberation is firmly in that person's hands. The same applies to the feeding prescribed for the Rahasya Nāmasāhasra.
contentBlocks.meanings: तस्यैव (tasyaiva) – for that person alone (tasya:of that person | eva:alone); सफलं (saphalaṃ) – fruitful; जन्म (janma) – birth; मुक्तिः (muktiḥ) – liberation; तस्य (tasya) – of that person; करे (kare) – in the hands; स्थिरा (sthirā) – firm; रहस्यनामसाहस्रभोजनेऽपि (rahasyanāmasāhasrabhōjanē'pi) – in the feeding of the secret thousand names (rahasya:secret | nāma:name | sāhasra:thousand | bhōjanē:in the feeding | api:also); एवमेव (evameva) – in the same way (evam:thus | eva:indeed); हि (hi) – indeed;

contentBlocks.original:
आदौ नित्यबलिं कुर्यात्पश्चाद्ब्राह्मणभोजनम् ।
रहस्यनामसाहस्रमहिमा यो मयोदितः ॥ ४२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādau nityabaliṃ kuryātpaśchādbrāhmaṇabhōjanam ।
rahasyanāmasāhasramahimā yō mayōditaḥ ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: First one should make the daily ritual offering, then feed the brāhmaṇas. The greatness of the Rahasya Nāmasāhasra that I have described...
contentBlocks.meanings: आदौ (ādau) – first; नित्यबलिं (nityabaliṃ) – daily ritual offering (nitya:daily | baliṃ:offering); कुर्यात् (kuryāt) – should make; पश्चात् (paścāt) – then; ब्राह्मणभोजनम् (brāhmaṇabhōjanam) – feeding the brāhmaṇas (brāhmaṇa:brāhmaṇas | bhōjanam:feeding); रहस्यनामसाहस्रमहिमा (rahasyanāmasāhasramahimā) – greatness of Rahasya Nāmasāhasra (rahasya:secret | nāma:name | sāhasra:thousand | mahimā:greatness); यः (yaḥ) – which; मया (mayā) – by me; उदितः (uditaḥ) – described;

contentBlocks.original:
स शीकराणुरत्नैकनाम्नो महिमवारिधेः ।
वाग्देवीरचिते नामसाहस्रे यद्यदीरितम् ॥ ४३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sa śīkarāṇuratnaikanāmnō mahimavāridhēḥ ।
vāgdēvīrachitē nāmasāhasrē yadyadīritam ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...is but a single drop from the vast ocean of the glory of even a single name here. Whatever has been declared as the fruit of the Vāgdevī-composed Thousand Names...
contentBlocks.meanings: स (sa) – that; शीकराणु (śīkarāṇu) – drop; रत्न (ratna) – jewel; एक (eka) – one; नाम्नः (nāmnaḥ) – of the name; महिमा (mahimā) – glory; वारिधेः (vāridheḥ) – of the ocean; वाग्देवी (vāgdevī) – Vāgdevī; रचिते (racite) – composed; नामसाहस्रे (nāmasāhasre) – in the thousand names (nāma:name | sāhasre:in the thousand); यद्यत् (yadyat) – whatever; ईरितम् (īritam) – declared;

contentBlocks.original:
तत्फलं कोटिगुणितं नाम्नोऽप्येकस्य कीर्तनात् ।
एतदन्यैर्जपैः स्तोत्रैरर्चनैर्यत्फलं भवेत् ॥ ४४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatphalaṃ kōṭiguṇitaṃ nāmnō'pyēkasya kīrtanāt ।
ētadanyairjapaiḥ stōtrairarchanairyatphalaṃ bhavēt ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...that same fruit, multiplied a crore-fold, comes from reciting even a single name here. Whatever fruit is obtained from other japas, hymns, and worship practices...
contentBlocks.meanings: तत्फलम् (tatphalam) – that fruit (tat:that | phalam:fruit); कोटिगुणितम् (koṭiguṇitam) – multiplied a crore-fold (koṭi:crore | guṇitam:multiplied); नाम्नः (nāmnaḥ) – of the name; अपि (api) – even; एकस्य (ekasya) – of one; कीर्तनात् (kīrtanāt) – from reciting; एतत् (etat) – this; अन्यैः (anyaiḥ) – by others; जपैः (japaiḥ) – by chants; स्तोत्रैः (stotraiḥ) – by hymns; अर्चनैः (arcanaiḥ) – by worship practices; यत् (yat) – whatever; फलम् (phalam) – fruit; भवेत् (bhavet) – may be;

contentBlocks.original:
तत्फलं कोटिगुणितं भवेन्नामशतत्रयात् ।
वाग्देवीरचिते स्तोत्रे तादृशो महिमा यदि ॥ ४५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tatphalaṃ kōṭiguṇitaṃ bhavēnnāmaśatatrayāt ।
vāgdēvīrachitē stōtrē tādṛśō mahimā yadi ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...that fruit, multiplied a crore-fold, comes from the Nāma-śata-traya (the three hundred names). If such is the greatness found in the Vāgdevī-composed stotra...
contentBlocks.meanings: तत्फलम् (tatphalam) – that fruit (tat:that | phalam:fruit); कोटिगुणितम् (koṭiguṇitam) – multiplied a crore-fold (koṭi:crore | guṇitam:multiplied); भवेत् (bhavet) – comes; नामशतत्रयात् (nāmaśatatrayāt) – from the three hundred names (nāma:names | śata:hundred | trayāt:from three); वाग्देवीरचिते (vāgdevīracite) – Vāgdevī-composed (vāgdevī:Vāgdevī | racite:composed); स्तोत्रे (stotre) – in the stotra; तादृशः (tādṛśaḥ) – such; महिमा (mahimā) – greatness; यदि (yadi) – if;

contentBlocks.original:
साक्षात्कामेशकामेशीकृतेऽस्मिन्गृह्यतामिति ।
सकृत्सङ्कीर्तनादेव नाम्नामस्मिन् शतत्रये ॥ ४६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sākṣātkāmēśakāmēśīkṛtē'smingṛhyatāmiti ।
sakṛtsaṅkīrtanādēva nāmnāmasmin śatatrayē ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...how much more so in this one, composed directly by Kāmeśa and Kāmeśī themselves? From even a single recitation of any name in this three-hundred-name stotra...
contentBlocks.meanings: साक्षात् (sākṣāt) – directly; कामेशकामेशीकृते (kāmeśakāmeśīkṛte) – composed by Kāmeśa and Kāmeśī (kāmeśa:Kāmeśa | kāmeśī:Kāmeśī | kṛte:composed by); अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; गृह्यताम् (gṛhyatām) – let it be accepted; इति (iti) – thus; सकृत् (sakṛt) – once; सङ्कीर्तनात् (saṅkīrtanāt) – from recitation; एव (eva) – indeed; नाम्नाम् (nāmnām) – of names; अस्मिन् (asmin) – in this; शतत्रये (śatatraye) – in the three hundred (śata:hundred | traye:in the three);

contentBlocks.original:
भवेच्चित्तस्य पर्याप्तिर्न्यूनमन्यानपेक्षिणी ।
न ज्ञातव्यमितोऽप्यन्यत्र जप्तव्यं च कुम्भज ॥ ४७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavēchchittasya paryāptirnyūnamanyānapēkṣiṇī ।
na jñātavyamitō'pyanyatra japtavyaṃ cha kumbhaja ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...the heart attains a fullness that seeks nothing further. Therefore, O Kumbhaja, you need not seek any other knowledge, nor practise any other japa.
contentBlocks.meanings: भवेत् (bhavet) – may become; चित्तस्य (cittasya) – of the mind; पर्याप्तिः (paryāptiḥ) – fullness; न्यूनम् (nyūnam) – deficient; अन्यानपेक्षिणी (anyānapekṣiṇī) – not dependent on others (anya:others | anapekṣiṇī:not dependent); न (na) – not; ज्ञातव्यम् (jñātavyam) – to be known; इतः (itaḥ) – from here; अपि (api) – also; अन्यत्र (anyatra) – elsewhere; जप्तव्यम् (japtavyam) – to be chanted; च (ca) – and; कुम्भज (kumbhaja) – O Kumbhaja;

contentBlocks.original:
यद्यत्साध्यतमं कार्यं तत्तदर्थमिदं जपेत् ।
तत्तत्फलमवाप्नोति पश्चात्कार्यं परीक्षयेत् ॥ ४८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadyatsādhyatamaṃ kāryaṃ tattadarthamidaṃ japēt ।
tattatphalamavāpnōti paśchātkāryaṃ parīkṣayēt ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whatever great goal you wish to accomplish, recite this for the sake of that goal. You will attain that result — then examine the proof for yourself.
contentBlocks.meanings: यद्यत् (yadyat) – whatever (yat:whatever | yat:whatever); साध्यतमं (sādhyatamaṃ) – most achievable (sādhya:achievable | tama:most); कार्यं (kāryaṃ) – goal; तत्तदर्थम् (tattadartham) – for that purpose (tat:that | artha:purpose); इदं (idaṃ) – this; जपेत् (japet) – recite; तत्तत्फलम् (tattatphalam) – that result (tat:that | phalam:result); अवाप्नोति (avāpnoti) – attains; पश्चात् (paścāt) – then; कार्यं (kāryaṃ) – goal; परीक्षयेत् (parīkṣayet) – examine;

contentBlocks.original:
ये ये प्रयोगास्तन्त्रेषु तैस्तैर्यत्साध्यते फलम् ।
तत्सर्वं सिध्यति क्षिप्रं नामत्रिशतकीर्तनात् ॥ ४९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yē yē prayōgāstantrēṣu taistairyatsādhyatē phalam ।
tatsarvaṃ sidhyati kṣipraṃ nāmatriśatakīrtanāt ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Whatever practices are prescribed in the Tantras and whatever fruit they yield — all of that is swiftly accomplished through reciting this stotra of three hundred names.
contentBlocks.meanings: ये (ye) – whatever; ये (ye) – whatever; प्रयोगाः (prayogāḥ) – practices; तन्त्रेषु (tantreṣu) – in the Tantras; तैः (taiḥ) – by those; तैः (taiḥ) – by those; यत् (yat) – whatever; साध्यते (sādhyate) – is accomplished; फलम् (phalam) – fruit; तत् (tat) – that; सर्वम् (sarvam) – all; सिध्यति (sidhyati) – is accomplished; क्षिप्रं (kṣipraṃ) – swiftly; नामत्रिशतकीर्तनात् (nāmatriśatakīrtanāt) – through reciting three hundred names (nāma:name | triśata:three hundred | kīrtanāt:through reciting);

contentBlocks.original:
आयुष्करं पुष्टिकरं पुत्रदं वश्यकारकम् ।
विद्याप्रदं कीर्तिकरं सुकवित्वप्रदायकम् ॥ ५० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āyuṣkaraṃ puṣṭikaraṃ putradaṃ vaśyakārakam ।
vidyāpradaṃ kīrtikaraṃ sukavitvapradāyakam ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: It bestows longevity, nourishment, progeny, power of attraction, knowledge, fame, and the gift of eloquent poetry...
contentBlocks.meanings: आयुष्करं (āyuṣkaraṃ) – bestows longevity (āyuṣ:longevity | karaṃ:bestows); पुष्टिकरं (puṣṭikaraṃ) – bestows nourishment (puṣṭi:nourishment | karaṃ:bestows); पुत्रदं (putradaṃ) – grants progeny (putra:progeny | daṃ:grants); वश्यकारकम् (vaśyakārakam) – power of attraction (vaśya:attraction | kārakam:power of); विद्याप्रदं (vidyāpradaṃ) – grants knowledge (vidyā:knowledge | pradaṃ:grants); कीर्तिकरं (kīrtikaraṃ) – bestows fame (kīrti:fame | karaṃ:bestows); सुकवित्वप्रदायकम् (sukavitvapradāyakam) – gift of eloquent poetry (su:eloquent | kavitva:poetry | pradāyakam:gift of);

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वसम्पत्प्रदं सर्वभोगदं सर्वसौख्यदम् ।
सर्वाभीष्टप्रदं चैव देव्या नामशतत्रयम् ॥ ५१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvasampatpradaṃ sarvabhōgadaṃ sarvasaukhyadam ।
sarvābhīṣṭapradaṃ chaiva dēvyā nāmaśatatrayam ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...all wealth, all enjoyment, all happiness, the fulfilment of every wish — all this is granted by the Goddess's three hundred names.
contentBlocks.meanings: सर्वसम्पत्प्रदं (sarvasampatpradaṃ) – grants all wealth (sarva:all | sampat:wealth | pradaṃ:grants); सर्वभोगदं (sarvabhōgadaṃ) – grants all enjoyment (sarva:all | bhōga:enjoyment | daṃ:grants); सर्वसौख्यदम् (sarvasaukhyadam) – grants all happiness (sarva:all | saukhyam:happiness | dam:grants); सर्वाभीष्टप्रदं (sarvābhīṣṭapradaṃ) – grants all wishes (sarva:all | abhīṣṭa:wishes | pradaṃ:grants); च (ca) – and; एव (eva) – indeed; देव्या (devyā) – by the Goddess; नामशतत्रयम् (nāmaśatatrayam) – three hundred names (nāma:names | śata:hundred | trayam:three);

contentBlocks.original:
एतज्जपपरो भूयान्नान्यदिच्छेत्कदाचन ।
एतत्कीर्तनसन्तुष्टा श्रीदेवी ललिताम्बिका ॥ ५२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētajjapaparō bhūyānnānyadichChētkadāchana ।
ētatkīrtanasantuṣṭā śrīdēvī lalitāmbikā ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Let one be devoted to this recitation and never desire anything else. Pleased by this recitation, Śrī Devī Lalitāmbikā...
contentBlocks.meanings: एतत् (etat) – this; जप (japa) – recitation; परः (paraḥ) – devoted; भूयात् (bhūyāt) – let one be; न (na) – not; अन्यत् (anyat) – anything else; इच्छेत् (icchet) – desire; कदाचन (kadācana) – ever; एतत् (etat) – this; कीर्तन (kīrtana) – recitation; सन्तुष्टा (santuṣṭā) – pleased; श्रीदेवी (śrīdevī) – Śrī Devī (śrī:auspicious | devī:goddess); ललिताम्बिका (lalitāmbikā) – Lalitāmbikā (lalitā:graceful | ambikā:mother);

contentBlocks.original:
भक्तस्य यद्यदिष्टं स्यात्तत्तत्पूरयते ध्रुवम् ।
तस्मात्कुम्भोद्भव मुने कीर्तय त्वमिदं सदा ॥ ५३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhaktasya yadyadiṣṭaṃ syāttattatpūrayatē dhruvam ।
tasmātkumbhōdbhava munē kīrtaya tvamidaṃ sadā ॥ 53 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: ...certainly fulfils all the desires of Her devotee. Therefore, O Kumbhodbhava, the sage, always recite this.
contentBlocks.meanings: भक्तस्य (bhaktasya) – of the devotee; यद्यत् (yadyat) – whatever; इष्टं (iṣṭaṃ) – desired; स्यात् (syāt) – may be; तत्तत् (tattat) – that; पूरयते (pūrayate) – fulfils; ध्रुवम् (dhruvam) – certainly; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – therefore; कुम्भोद्भव (kumbhodbhava) – O Kumbhodbhava (kumbha:pot | udbhava:born from); मुने (mune) – O sage; कीर्तय (kīrtaya) – recite; त्वम् (tvam) – you; इदं (idaṃ) – this; सदा (sadā) – always;

contentBlocks.original:
नापरं किञ्चिदपि ते बोद्धव्यमवशिष्यते ।
इति ते कथितं स्तोत्रं ललिताप्रीतिदायकम् ॥ ५४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāparaṃ kiñchidapi tē bōddhavyamavaśiṣyatē ।
iti tē kathitaṃ stōtraṃ lalitāprītidāyakam ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Nothing else remains for you to know. This stotra, bestowing the love of Lalitā, has been told to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; अपरं (aparaṃ) – other; किञ्चित् (kiñcit) – anything; अपि (api) – even; ते (te) – for you; बोद्धव्यम् (boddhavyam) – to be known; अवशिष्यते (avaśiṣyate) – remains; इति (iti) – thus; ते (te) – to you; कथितं (kathitaṃ) – told; स्तोत्रं (stotraṃ) – hymn; ललिताप्रीतिदायकम् (lalitāprītidāyakam) – bestowing Lalitā's love (lalitā:Lalitā | prīti:love | dāyakam:bestowing);

contentBlocks.original:
नाविद्यावेदिने ब्रूयान्नाभक्ताय कदाचन ।
न शठाय न दुष्टाय नाविश्वासाय कर्हिचित् ॥ ५६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nāvidyāvēdinē brūyānnābhaktāya kadāchana ।
na śaṭhāya na duṣṭāya nāviśvāsāya karhichit ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Do not tell this to one who is not a knower of the Vidyā, nor to one who is not a devotee, nor to the deceitful, nor to the wicked, nor to the untrustworthy — under any circumstances.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; अविद्यावेदिने (avidyāvedine) – to one not knowing Vidyā (avidyā:not knowing Vidyā | vedine:to one knowing); ब्रूयात् (brūyāt) – should tell; न (na) – not; अभक्ताय (abhaktāya) – to one not devoted (a:not | bhaktāya:to the devoted); कदाचन (kadācana) – at any time; न (na) – not; शठाय (śaṭhāya) – to the deceitful; न (na) – not; दुष्टाय (duṣṭāya) – to the wicked; न (na) – not; अविश्वासाय (aviśvāsāya) – to the untrustworthy (a:not | viśvāsāya:to the trustworthy); कर्हिचित् (karhicit) – under any circumstances;

contentBlocks.original:
यो ब्रूयात्त्रिशतीं नाम्नां तस्यानर्थो महान्भवेत् ।
इत्याज्ञा शाङ्करी प्रोक्ता तस्माद्गोप्यमिदं त्वया ॥ ५७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yō brūyāttriśatīṃ nāmnāṃ tasyānarthō mahānbhavēt ।
ityājñā śāṅkarī prōktā tasmādgōpyamidaṃ tvayā ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One who reveals these Trishatī names to the unworthy will face great misfortune. This is the command of Śaṅkara — therefore guard this carefully.
contentBlocks.meanings: यः (yaḥ) – who; ब्रूयात् (brūyāt) – should speak; त्रिशतीं (triśatīm) – three hundred; नाम्नाम् (nāmnām) – of names; तस्य (tasya) – his; अनर्थः (anarthaḥ) – misfortune; महान् (mahān) – great; भवेत् (bhavet) – will be; इति (iti) – thus; आज्ञा (ājñā) – command; शाङ्करी (śāṅkarī) – of Śaṅkara; प्रोक्ता (proktā) – spoken; तस्मात् (tasmāt) – therefore; गोप्यम् (gopyam) – to be guarded; इदं (idaṃ) – this; त्वया (tvayā) – by you;

contentBlocks.original:
ललिताप्रेरितेनैव मयोक्तं स्तोत्रमुत्तमम् ।
रहस्यनामसाहस्रादपि गोप्यमिदं मुने ॥ ५८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lalitāprēritēnaiva mayōktaṃ stōtramuttamam ।
rahasyanāmasāhasrādapi gōpyamidaṃ munē ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This supreme stotra has been spoken by me only at the direct inspiration of Lalitā herself. O sage, this is to be kept even more secret than the Rahasya Nāmasāhasra.
contentBlocks.meanings: ललिताप्रेरितेनैव (lalitāprēritēnaiva) – by Lalitā's inspiration (lalitā:Lalitā | prēritēna:by inspiration | eva:only); मया (mayā) – by me; उक्तं (uktaṃ) – spoken; स्तोत्रम् (stotram) – hymn; उत्तमम् (uttamam) – supreme; रहस्यनामसाहस्रादपि (rahasyanāmasāhasrādapi) – than the secret thousand names (rahasya:secret | nāma:names | sāhasrāt:from thousand | api:even); गोप्यम् (gopyam) – to be kept secret; इदं (idaṃ) – this; मुने (munē) – O sage;

contentBlocks.original:
सूत उवाच ।
एवमुक्त्वा हयग्रीवः कुम्भजं तापसोत्तमम् ।
स्तोत्रेणानेन ललितां स्तुत्वा त्रिपुरसुन्दरीम् ।
आनन्दलहरीमग्नमानसः समवर्तत ॥ ५९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sūta uvācha ।
ēvamuktvā hayagrīvaḥ kumbhajaṃ tāpasōttamam ।
stōtrēṇānēna lalitāṃ stutvā tripurasundarīm ।
ānandalaharīmagnamānasaḥ samavartata ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Sūta said: Having thus spoken, Hayagrīva praised Lalitā Tripurasundarī with this stotra and was filled with supreme bliss. Agastya, his heart overflowing with devotion, bowed again and again to the lotus feet of Hayagrīva.
contentBlocks.meanings: सूत (sūta) – Sūta; उवाच (uvāca) – said; एवम् (evam) – thus; उक्त्वा (uktvā) – having spoken; हयग्रीवः (hayagrīvaḥ) – Hayagrīva; कुम्भजम् (kumbhajam) – Agastya; तापस (tāpasa) – sage; उत्तमम् (uttamam) – excellent; स्तोत्रेण (stotreṇa) – with hymn; अनेन (anena) – this; ललिताम् (lalitām) – Lalitā; स्तुत्वा (stutvā) – having praised; त्रिपुरसुन्दरीम् (tripurasundarīm) – Tripurasundarī (tripura:three cities | sundarī:beautiful goddess); आनन्दलहरीम् (ānandalaharīm) – wave of bliss (ānanda:bliss | laharī:wave); अग्नमानसः (agnamānasaḥ) – mind immersed (agna:immersed | mānasaḥ:mind); समवर्तत (samavartata) – was filled;

contentBlocks.original: इति ब्रह्माण्डपुराणे उत्तरखण्डे हयग्रीवागस्त्यसंवादे ललितोपाख्याने स्तोत्रखण्डे श्रीललितात्रिशतीस्तोत्ररत्नम् ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti brahmāṇḍapurāṇē uttarakhaṇḍē hayagrīvāgastyasaṃvādē lalitōpākhyānē stōtrakhaṇḍē śrīlalitātriśatīstōtraratnam ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus concludes the gem of the Śrī Lalitā Trishatī Stotra, from the Stotra section of the Lalitopākhyāna, in the dialogue between Hayagrīva and Agastya, in the Uttara Khaṇḍa of the Brahmāṇḍa Purāṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: इति (iti) – thus; ब्रह्माण्डपुराणे (brahmāṇḍapurāṇe) – in the Brahmāṇḍa Purāṇa (brahmāṇḍa:Brahmāṇḍa | purāṇe:in the Purāṇa); उत्तरखण्डे (uttarakhaṇḍe) – in the Uttara Khaṇḍa (uttara:upper | khaṇḍe:in the section); हयग्रीवागस्त्यसंवादे (hayagrīvāgastyasaṃvāde) – in the dialogue of Hayagrīva and Agastya (hayagrīva:Hayagrīva | agastya:Agastya | saṃvāde:in the dialogue); ललितोपाख्याने (lalitopākhyāne) – in the story of Lalitā (lalitā:Lalitā | upākhyāne:in the story); स्तोत्रखण्डे (stotrakhaṇḍe) – in the section of hymns (stotra:hymn | khaṇḍe:in the section); श्रीललितात्रिशतीस्तोत्ररत्नम् (śrīlalitātriśatīstotraratnam) – the gem of Śrī Lalitā Trishatī Stotra (śrī:auspicious | lalitā:Lalitā | triśatī:three hundred | stotra:hymn | ratnam:gem);
